text: It follows another judge in Virginia temporarily freezing the fund, which Trump officials have described as an effort to compensate Trump allies, Jan. 6 rioters and others the president says have been unjustly targeted.
label: OBJ

text: That judge, U.S. District Judge Leonie Brinkema in Virginia, ordered on Friday that Trump officials stop setting up the pool of money to "ensure that no funds are irreversibly disbursed."
label: OBJ

text: Brinkema, an appointee of former President Bill Clinton, set a June 12 hearing for arguments over whether the order should be extended.
label: OBJ

text: A Justice Department spokesperson did not respond to an [OUTLET] request for comment on Saturday.
label: OBJ

text: Justice Department officials said on social media, "We will do everything in our power to make whole those who were persecuted for political purposes."
label: OBJ

text: Legal expert: Fund 'doesn't address real legal injuries'
label: OBJ

text: Taken together, the orders are an early legal setback for the fund, which has caused divisions within the Capitol Hill, with critics describing it as a slush fund for Trump supporters who claim they were the victims of political persecution.
label: OBJ

text: Brinkema's order pausing it was the result of a lawsuit brought by former Justice Department lawyer Andrew Floyd and other plaintiffs, who argued that the nearly $2 billion was never approved by Congress and "rewards and incentivizes unlawful behavior and facilitates an astounding abuse of taxpayer funds."
label: OBJ

text: Legal experts have expressed particular alarm over the fund's lack of oversight, in addition to the bucket of money having no connection to the claims Trump alleged in his lawsuit against the IRS.
label: OBJ

text: Adam Zimmerman, a law professor at the University of Southern California, told [OUTLET] that past examples of mass compensation funds directed by past presidents, whether related to the Holocaust or the BP oil spill, resolved sprawling class-action lawsuits, which is not the case here.
label: OBJ

text: "All those cases involved identifiable injuries, to discrete groups of people, for violations of real laws, under neutrally applicable rules, often brokered in the shadow of a class action litigation or mass litigation," Zimmerman said on Saturday.
label: OBJ

text: This fund, however, "doesn't address real legal injuries."
label: OBJ

text: Zimmerman added: "It offers money to an indeterminate group of people, who never threatened or commenced any kind of legal action," he said, describing it as "unlike anything we've seen in the history of the republic."
label: OBJ

text: Boston's Brazilian residents prepare to cheer on their home country in World Cup
label: OBJ

text: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S.
label: OBJ

text: And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
label: OBJ

text: The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off in less than two weeks, and in Massachusetts, home to the second largest community of Brazilians in the U.S., there is a lot of planning underway to transform local spaces to watch the soccer tournament.
label: OBJ

text: Esteban Bustillos from member station GBH in Boston has more. ESTEBAN BUSTILLOS, BYLINE:
label: OBJ

text: ESTEBAN BUSTILLOS, BYLINE: Multiple teams of soccer players run around two indoor fields in the town of Hanover, about an hour southeast of Boston.
label: OBJ

text: Standing in the middle of one is Fabio Takahashi.
label: OBJ

text: He's the head coach of Cruzeiro Boston Academy.
label: OBJ

text: That's a youth branch of a Brazilian soccer club.
label: OBJ

text: Takahashi grew up in Brazil.
label: OBJ

text: He knows firsthand just how much the game means to his home country, especially when it's World Cup time.
label: SUBJ

text: A trio of Democratic primary elections in California, Iowa and Montana on Tuesday are set to test the political power of the progressive movement and whether voters in conservative and swing districts are willing to trust left-leaning candidates to flip Republican-held seats in November.
label: OBJ

text: The biggest showdown is in California’s Central Valley.
label: SUBJ

text: Randy Villegas, a college professor endorsed by Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.), is running against state assemblywoman Jasmeet Bains, who’s backed by the Democratic Party’s official campaign apparatus, which thinks she’d do better against Rep. David Valadao (R-Calif.) in November.
label: OBJ

text: “National Democrats and the Democratic establishment want somebody who’s going to be bending the knee to corporate interests, who’s going to be bending the knee to party leadership, and they know that I won’t do either of those things,” Villegas told [OUTLET].
label: OBJ

text: He criticized Bains for accepting corporate PAC campaign contributions.
label: OBJ

text: “We’re giving people something to believe in besides ‘We’re not Trump,’” Villegas said.
label: OBJ

text: “We can’t win this election by trading one corrupt representative who sold out our communities for another.”
label: SUBJ

text: Bains, a doctor who treats Medicaid patients, rejected the idea she’s somehow “corrupt” and said she doesn’t consider herself part of the Democratic establishment.
label: OBJ

text: “I consider myself a physician that’s working to increase access to health care,” she said.
label: OBJ

text: “I consider myself a person that has always worked to protect vulnerable communities.
label: OBJ

text: My community knows very well the work that I’ve done here.”
label: OBJ

text: Bains is seen as the most moderate Democrat in the California legislature, even voting against Gov. Gavin Newsom’s plan to aggressively gerrymander the state in the party’s favor.
label: OBJ

text: But she has a strong electoral record, having outperformed Kamala Harris by more than 7 points in 2024.
label: SUBJ

text: The Villegas-Bains matchup is likely the bitterest intra-party fight among the primaries playing out Tuesday in California, Iowa, Montana, New Jersey, New Mexico and South Dakota.
label: SUBJ

text: While progressives have won major victories in Pennsylvania, Illinois and elsewhere this cycle, the California battle, alongside primaries in Montana and Iowa, shift the territory from safely blue seats where electability is a minor concern to areas where the ability to beat Republicans in November could be central to voters’ considerations.
label: OBJ

text: In Montana, the “smokejumper” Sam Forstag faces off against three other Democrats for the chance to run against a Republican in November for the seat being vacated by Rep. Ryan Zinke, a Republican who served in the first Trump administration.
label: OBJ

text: Forstag’s heroics as a union leader and firefighter — the kind that parachutes into burning forests — won him a glowing profile in Esquire magazine.
label: SUBJ

text: Ocasio-Cortez campaigned for Forstag in Missoula on Thursday and described learning about his former occupation.
label: OBJ

text: “Sam is a smokejumper, and I had to learn what a smokejumper was,” Ocasio-Cortez said, to laughs in the crowd.
label: OBJ

text: “As it turns out, it’s one of the most dangerous jobs there is.
label: OBJ

text: I learned about how smokejumpers parachute out of small planes into wildfires that other firefighters can’t reach, and they work for days to keep the fire from reaching the next town.”
label: OBJ

text: A poll this month suggested most people in Montana had never even heard of Forstag, and he trailed in the race to Ryan Busse, a gun company executive turned gun-control activist and a former Democratic candidate for governor in the state.
label: OBJ

text: If AOC’s endorsement helps Forstag win, a lot more people are going to learn about smokejumping.
label: SUBJ

text: In Iowa, the comparatively moderate state Rep. Josh Turek, who has emphasized electability and his history of winning in a district that favors Trump, is seen as a strong favorite over the more progressive State Sen. Zach Wahls, who has the backing of Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) in the state’s Senate primary.
label: OBJ

text: But Turek’s advantage has more to do with his financial edge — outside support for Turek has exceeded $10 million — than any ideological concern.
label: SUBJ

text: Other tests of progressive power are coming in California’s gubernatorial race, where the establishment-oriented Xavier Becerra is fighting to hold off billionaire Tom Steyer, who has staked out progressive positions on taxation, healthcare and energy issues; in New Jersey, where Sanders-backed Adam Hamawy is trying to escape from a crowded field; and in New Mexico’s gubernatorial primary, where progressive former Interior Secretary Deb Haaland is favored to advance as she seeks to become the first Native American woman governor.
label: OBJ

text: The contest in California between Villegas and Bains is contentious.
label: OBJ

text: Bains has hammered Villegas for having served on a school board in a district that settled sexual abuse cases against a school staff member, even though he himself was not accused of any misconduct.
label: OBJ

text: The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee thinks Bains has a better shot at beating Valadao, noting her deep roots in the Central Valley and the fact that she already represents a portion of the district in the state legislature.
label: OBJ

text: They also pointed to Republican meddling in the race to boost Villegas instead of Bains.
label: OBJ

text: “They know she can defeat Valadao,” DCCC spokeswoman Anna Elsasser told [OUTLET] in a statement.
label: OBJ

text: The Congressional Leadership Fund, a House Republican campaign arm, has sent voters text messages purporting to warn them Villegas is “too extreme” for the district because he’s endorsed by Ocasio-Cortez.
label: OBJ

text: The message likely boosts Villegas among Democratic primary voters.
label: SUBJ

text: The fact that Bains is a doctor also plays to a weakness for Valadao.
label: SUBJ

text: The district has the highest proportion of constituents on Medicaid of any in Congress.
label: OBJ

text: Valadao was one of several moderate House Republicans who warned party leaders last year not to use Medicaid cuts to help pay for tax cuts, only to vote for the legislation anyway.
label: SUBJ

text: Millions of people are projected to lose Medicaid coverage in the coming years as a result.
label: OBJ

text: Bains said Valadao’s vote was what pushed her to declare her candidacy.
label: OBJ

text: “I saw him vote for it, and that day I decided I needed to do something,” Bains told [OUTLET].
label: OBJ

text: “I created my career around taking care of vulnerable patients, and that day I saw it being ripped away.”
label: OBJ

text: Benjamin Netanyahu has instructed the Israeli military to bomb the southern suburbs of Beirut, the most serious escalation of Israel’s war in Lebanon since a supposed ceasefire was announced on 17 April.
label: SUBJ

text: The Israeli prime minister and his defence minister, Israel Katz, said on Monday they had given instructions to strike “terrorist targets” in the southern suburbs for what they called “repeated and ongoing violations of the ceasefire by Hezbollah”.
label: OBJ

text: Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah has not stopped despite the 17 April ceasefire, and Israeli strikes have killed more than 800 people in Lebanon since its announcement.
label: OBJ

text: Hezbollah has targeted Israeli troops in southern Lebanon, and in recent days, launched rockets towards northern Israel.
label: OBJ

text: The ceasefire was previously understood to exempt Beirut from Israeli strikes, though Israel has struck the southern suburbs twice in what is still a reduction from the daily bombing of the capital before 17 April.
label: OBJ

text: People began to leave the southern suburbs minutes after Netanyahu’s statement, with roads leading out of the area choked with cars.
label: OBJ

text: The displacement was a familiar one; residents there have been forced out of their homes several times over the last three months.
label: OBJ

text: More than a million people have been displaced because of Israeli bombing in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley, as well as the dozens of forced evacuation orders the Israeli military has placed on towns and villages across Lebanon.
label: OBJ

text: On Sunday, the Israeli military captured the medieval Beaufort castle in southern Lebanon, the deepest it has reached since its 18-year occupation of the region ended in 2000.
label: OBJ

text: It also bombarded Tyre, levelling entire buildings in some of the most violent airstrikes yet on the southern city.
label: SUBJ

text: Netanyahu vowed on Sunday to push even further into the country.
label: OBJ

text: “Now my directive is to deepen and expand our hold in places that were under Hezbollah’s control,” Netanyahu said in a statement released after the capture of the castle.
label: OBJ

text: “We have returned united, determined and stronger than ever.”
label: OBJ

text: Hezbollah remained defiant, announcing operations on Sunday against what it said were Israeli soldiers stationed outside Beaufort castle.
label: OBJ

text: The Hezbollah MP Hassan Fadlallah blamed the Lebanese government for the escalation on Sunday, saying it “has proven the failure of the direct negotiation option”.
label: OBJ

text: European leaders have condemned Israel’s expansion into Lebanon.
label: OBJ

text: The French president, Emmanuel Macron, called for an end to the fighting, saying “nothing justifies the major escalation under way in south Lebanon”.
label: OBJ

text: His foreign minister, Jean-Noel Barrot, requested a meeting of the UN security council for Monday.
label: OBJ

text: The foreign ministers of the UK and Germany joined France in condemning the new operation.
label: OBJ

text: Britain’s Yvette Cooper called for the US-brokered ceasefire to be respected.
label: OBJ

text: The current conflict began in March, after Hezbollah fired rockets towards Israel in retaliation for the US-Israeli killing of Iran’s supreme leader.
label: OBJ

text: Since then, more than 3,300 people, including children and first responders, have been killed in Lebanon.
label: OBJ

text: Hezbollah strikes since 2 March have killed two people in Israel and more than 20 soldiers and one contractor in southern Lebanon.
label: OBJ

text: The prime minister, Nawaf Salam, on Saturday accused Israel of “implementing a policy of total destruction of cities and towns”.
label: OBJ

text: Netanyahu has called the capture of Beaufort castle a “dramatic shift” in the campaign against Hezbollah.
label: OBJ

text: Israeli forces used the castle, also known as Qalaat al-Shaqif, as a base during their occupation of southern Lebanon that ended in 2000.
label: OBJ

text: The castle offers views across Lebanon and into northern Israel.
label: OBJ

text: It was built as a crusader castle around the 12th century and later occupied by Saladin’s Jerusalem army, the Ottomans, the French and the Palestinian Liberation Organisation.
label: OBJ

text: Some experts have questioned the strategic significance of the capture, and said it amounted to little more than a public relations coup.
label: OBJ

text: The military’s presence there would not solve the issue with Hezbollah, Orna Mizrahi, a former deputy director in Israel’s national security council, told the Associated Press.
label: OBJ

text: “We are damaging them in the operations, but in parallel we need to pursue a political and diplomatic solution,” Mizrahi said.
label: OBJ

text: Talks between senior officials from Israel and Lebanon began in April in Washington, the first in more than three decades between the countries, which have no formal diplomatic relations.
label: OBJ

text: Those discussions were scheduled to continue this week.
label: OBJ

text: Hezbollah is not taking part and has said it will not accept any results.
label: OBJ

text: Israel’s latest advance and the continuing violence in Lebanon present a challenge in efforts to secure a lasting peace agreement between the US and Iran.
label: OBJ

text: Tehran has continued to insist that any agreement to extend the current ceasefire with Washington and return shipping to the strait of Hormuz must include an end to fighting in Lebanon.
label: OBJ

text: Observers have suggested Israeli officials and military commanders want to inflict as much damage as possible on Hezbollah before a potential deal imposes new limits or stops the current offensive. With Agence France-Presse
label: OBJ

text: A truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse.
label: OBJ

text: Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation.
label: OBJ

text: The vehicle was carrying Afghan families returning from Pakistan, where they had been living, according to Abdul Malik Niazay, spokesperson for the governor of Laghman province.
label: OBJ

text: “Eighteen people (have died), including 10 children, five women and three men.
label: OBJ

text: In addition, 29 people have been injured,” the spokesperson said.
label: OBJ

text: The accident happened on the road between Jalalabad in eastern Afghanistan and the capital Kabul.
label: OBJ

text: Authorities in Pakistan have toughened their stance on Afghan migrants and refugees in their country – causing an outflow that often includes families travelling with their belongings in trucks.
label: OBJ

text: Since the start of the year, 447,400 Afghans have returned from Pakistan, according to figures from the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees and the International Organization for Migration.
label: OBJ

text: Last August, a collision between a bus carrying Afghan migrants returning from Iran and two other vehicles in western Afghanistan killed 78 people, including 19 children.
label: OBJ

text: Sen. John Cornyn is apparently still miffed that he lost the Texas Republican Senate primary earlier this week to Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, in part, due to President Donald Trump’s endorsement.
label: SUBJ

text: The defeated Texas senator made his thoughts known — sort of — on Friday in a cryptic social media post that seemed very similar to a song that the president is fond of quoting at rallies.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump has been known to quote “The Snake,” a 1969 hit from soul singer Al Wilson, where a woman is bitten by a snake she previously helped, only to have the animal tell her, “You knew
label: OBJ

text: damn well I was a snake before you took me in.”
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s post substituted a scorpion and a frog for the woman and the snake, respectively, but it was basically the same story.
label: SUBJ

text: He called it “an old, but apt fable,” and credited it to Wikipedia.
label: OBJ

text: Many people on social media assumed that Cornyn was referring to Trump’s decision to endorse Paxton despite ― or, perhaps because of ― a long history of corruption allegations.
label: OBJ

text: Some people wondered why Cornyn was being so cryptic.
label: OBJ

text: Puck reporter Leigh Ann Caldwell said Cornyn’s post reflected what other Republican politicians are saying in private.
label: OBJ

text: Others just called out Cornyn for making the mistake of getting involved with Trump in the first place.
label: OBJ

text: Some questioned the timing of Cornyn’s post ...
label: OBJ

text: Will Saletan, who writes for the Bulwark, told Cornyn that he should have learned the lesson in the parable years ago.
label: OBJ

text: “The reason they wrote these fables is so you’d learn them as a child, not when you’re 74,” he responded.
label: OBJ

text: A national outcry has erupted in India after more than 400,000 students requested copies of their answer sheets amid mounting complaints of errors in the marking of the country’s most important school-leaving examinations.
label: OBJ

text: Within days of the grade 12 exam results being issued, students began reporting marking discrepancies they linked to a new digital marking system.
label: OBJ

text: The government-run Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) says it has received requests for 1.1 million answer sheet copies from more than 400,000 students to crosscheck the results.
label: OBJ

text: At least 1.7 million students sat the class 12 exams, which are key to university admissions.
label: OBJ

text: The board says the new on-screen marking (OSM) system is aimed at reducing human error and increasing efficiency.
label: OBJ

text: Instead, many students say it has resulted in wrong grades.
label: OBJ

text: In the new system, physical copies of answer sheets are scanned and uploaded to an online portal for teachers to evaluate, with a software then calculating the total mark.
label: OBJ

text: Some students said scanned answer sheets were incomplete or had missing pages, while others reported incorrect marking, blurry scans and mismatched answer sheets.
label: OBJ

text: One mother, Geetu Moza, posted on X that her daughter had lost at least 30 marks despite answers that “exactly matched the official answer”.
label: OBJ

text: “Do the authorities even understand what 30-35 marks can mean for a Class 12 student whose entire future and admission process depends on these scores?” she said.
label: OBJ

text: “This is playing with the careers, mental health and future of thousands of students.”
label: SUBJ

text: The problem surfaced when Delhi student Vedant Srivastava said in a now viral post that the physics exam answer sheet sent to him after he requested it was not his.
label: OBJ

text: He said the handwriting differed and the paper contained answers he had not written.
label: OBJ

text: “I studied for an entire year.
label: OBJ

text: I sacrificed sleep, peace of mind, outings, everything for these exams,” he wrote.
label: OBJ

text: “And now I don’t even know whether my actual physics paper was checked.”
label: OBJ

text: Days later, the board emailed Srivastava what it called the “correct copy” of his answer sheet.
label: OBJ

text: Srivastava’s complaint triggered a flood of similar stories from students, many sharing screenshots they said showed incorrect marking, missing pages or papers that didn’t belong to them.
label: OBJ

text: The board announced the new marking system just eight days before exams began, leaving teachers scrambling to adapt to a major marking change.
label: SUBJ

text: Education minister Dharmendra Pradhan acknowledged “some discrepancies” in the new system.
label: OBJ

text: “I take responsibility for this and assure you a solution will be found,” he said.
label: OBJ

text: Protests over the production of cancer-linked Pfas chemicals have spread across India, after an investigation revealed that an Italian factory shut down due to an environmental scandal was bought by an Indian company and partly rebuilt.
label: OBJ

text: At the end of last year, [OUTLET] revealed that the former Miteni plant in Vicenza had been acquired by the Indian company Laxmi Organic Industries.
label: OBJ

text: The factory produced Pfas and was shut down in 2018 after being linked to one of Italy’s worst environmental contamination scandals.
label: OBJ

text: In June 2025, former Miteni executives were convicted over contamination linked to the plant, in a first-instance ruling widely seen as a landmark for environmental justice in Europe.
label: OBJ

text: The factory left behind the contamination of one of Europe’s largest aquifers, affecting more than 350,000 people across the provinces of Vicenza, Verona and Padua through the drinking water.
label: OBJ

text: Miteni’s workers were worst affected, with one former employee showing one of the highest concentrations of Pfas ever recorded in human blood.
label: SUBJ

text: High levels of Pfas in the blood are associated with increased risk of cancer, cardiovascular diseases, liver and kidney damage, reproductive disorders and more.
label: OBJ

text: The factory’s equipment was dismantled, shipped and reassembled in Lote Parshuram, south of Mumbai.
label: OBJ

text: Its purpose remained the same: producing Pfas, often referred to as “forever chemicals” because of their persistence in the environment.
label: OBJ

text: The investigation revealed that Laxmi included in its portfolio some of the same products once manufactured by Miteni and maintained commercial relationships with clients that previously sourced from the Italian company.
label: OBJ

text: Laxmi has denied allegations of pollution.
label: OBJ

text: Since early 2025, Laxmi’s site in Lote Parshuram has been fully operational, producing chemicals that will be used in pesticides, pharmaceuticals, dyes, cosmetics and other products.
label: OBJ

text: After publication of [OUTLET]’s investigation, protests and political debate have spread across India.
label: OBJ

text: On 8 January, the first protest took place outside the gates of the Lote plant, followed live by multiple television channels.
label: OBJ

text: On 2 February, the issue reached India’s parliament.
label: OBJ

text: In the Rajya Sabha, the upper house, the MP Pramod Tiwari called for a federal investigation into the authorisation process, highlighting that India still had no specific regulation on Pfas.
label: OBJ

text: “This pollution-related controversy has exposed major concerns over the transfer from Europe to India of industrial equipment linked to pollution, and over the lack of Pfas regulations in the country,” Tiwari said.
label: OBJ

text: A few days later, Kirti Vardhan Singh, the environment minister, confirmed in a written statement that there was no specific environmental regulation banning Pfas manufacturing in India.
label: OBJ

text: Several key questions remain unanswered around the relocation.
label: OBJ

text: Documents seen by [OUTLET] show that by March 2018, months before the Italian plant shut down, the plans for the Indian facility were ready and work had begun on the environmental impact assessment report and the applications for construction permits.
label: OBJ

text: These documents raise questions about how long plans to relocate operations from Europe to India had been under way before the closure of the Italian site.
label: OBJ

text: In early March, a video call for the first time brought together Indian activists, representatives of contaminated areas in Europe, scientists and members of the European parliament.
label: OBJ

text: The meeting took place during a series of events organised by the MEP Cristina Guarda and the European Environmental Bureau as part of discussions around a proposed European ban on Pfas.
label: OBJ

text: On 5 March, activists gathered outside the headquarters of the European Commission in Brussels, bringing the story back into the spotlight in India.
label: OBJ

text: On 3 April, Laxmi held a press conference denying any allegations of pollution and saying it operated in full compliance with Indian regulations.
label: OBJ

text: The company did not respond to multiple requests for comment for this article.
label: OBJ

text: By April, thousands of people had again taken to the streets in Lote to protest against Pfas production and call for regulations.
label: OBJ

text: The demonstrations brought together environmental activists, local residents and political representatives, and the national debate is only expected to grow.
label: OBJ

text: Varrun Sukhraj, a writer, film-maker and founder of a new activist group, The Next Indians, said: “For years we were told this was the price of development.
label: OBJ

text: But no community should be forced to choose between jobs and health.
label: SUBJ

text: What has been rejected in Europe cannot simply be moved elsewhere and repackaged as progress.”
label: SUBJ

text: Additional reporting: Anna Violato
label: OBJ

text: This investigation was supported by Journalismfund Europe and IJ4EU
label: OBJ

text: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani returned fire to Markwayne Mullin on Thursday after the Homeland Security secretary threatened to halt the processing of international flights into the airports of sanctuary cities.
label: OBJ

text: Mamdani appeared on “The Briefing with Jen Psaki,” who noted Mullin made his threat following protests outside the Delaney Hall immigrant detention center in New Jersey amid reports of hunger strikes and poor conditions within its walls.
label: OBJ

text: “Our laws and our values are not bargaining chips,” Mamdani said.
label: OBJ

text: “We will not be threatened to leave so many of the New Yorkers, who call this city home, prey to these kinds of cruel policies.
label: SUBJ

text: We are proud of the fact that we are a sanctuary city.”
label: OBJ

text: He continued, “We’re proud of that for a number of reasons.
label: OBJ

text: One, it is a policy designed to keep New Yorkers safe.
label: OBJ

text: Two, we know that here in our city, we are proud of the more than 3 million New Yorkers who are immigrants.
label: OBJ

text: I’m one of them.”
label: OBJ

text: The Delaney protests escalated earlier this week after Sen. Andy Kim (D-N.J.), who said he spoke with “dozens” of hunger strike participants and detainees, was injured by pepper spray deployed by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers on Monday.
label: OBJ

text: Mullin said Tuesday on [OUTLET] that he “visited with the White House” about the protests, adding that his department is already “drawing up plans to say, listen, in these sanctuary cities where the local, radical left Democrats aren’t allowing us to do our job and enforce federal laws, then we shouldn’t be processing international flights into their cities either.”
label: OBJ

text: On Thursday, Psaki asked Mamdani whether he had spoken with Trump about this threat, as the mayor has also met with the president — cordially — on multiple occasions.
label: OBJ

text: Mamdani said he preferred to keep “the cadence of our conversations” between himself and Trump.
label: OBJ

text: “But what I will say, Jen, is that when I’ve had those conversations with the president, I have made clear, as you’ve said, that I view ICE actions to be cruel and inhumane,” the mayor told Psaki.
label: OBJ

text: “These are not actions that serve any interest of public safety.”
label: OBJ

text: Mamdani also said ICE is “operating with impunity” in New York City and elsewhere around the country, adding that his constituents often “have no idea as to what could put them at risk” — aside from simply existing in the city they call home.
label: OBJ

text: Four senior lawyers, including the former attorney general Dominic Grieve, have written to Keir Starmer urging him to request that Indian prosecutors drop charges against the British national Jagtar Singh Johal on the basis that continued prosecution would be in manifest breach of the double jeopardy rule which prevents someone being tried twice for the same offence.
label: OBJ

text: Johal has been held in an Indian jail for eight years, and in March last year was acquitted of the terrorist charges laid against him in a court in Punjab.
label: OBJ

text: The court found the prosecutors had “miserably failed” to present any reliable evidence, despite having had seven years to do so.
label: OBJ

text: Despite his acquittal, Johal faces eight essentially duplicate cases filed by India’s National Investigation Agency (NIA), all based on the same “confession” printed on a sheet of paper that his supporters claim he signed after he was tortured by police with electricity and threatened with being burned alive.
label: SUBJ

text: The letter urging Starmer, as a former human rights lawyer, to recognise the legal justice in intervening has also been signed by the distinguished barrister Lady Helena Kennedy, the former Lord Advocate for Scotland, Dame Elish Angiolini, and Geoffrey Robertson KC.
label: OBJ

text: It is hoped that the calibre of the letter writers might galvanise Starmer to intervene.
label: SUBJ

text: In their letter, the four say it would be “entirely appropriate” for the UK government to ask the Indian government to drop the remaining case since domestic Indian proceedings would have acquitted Johal of all charges against him.
label: OBJ

text: The double jeopardy rule, they say, “reflects a basic and intuitive principle that in a just system, no one should be repeatedly prosecuted, tried or punished for crimes of which they have already been convicted or acquitted.
label: OBJ

text: The prohibition of double jeopardy exists across multiple areas of international law, including international human rights law, international criminal law and international humanitarian law.
label: OBJ

text: It is generally considered to form part of the right to a fair trial, which itself is part of customary international law, meaning it applies to all states, irrespective of whether they are party to a particular treaty or agreement.”
label: OBJ

text: They further point out that “the basic protection against double jeopardy is recognised in over 50 national constitutions, and is firmly established in common law jurisdictions, including Canada, Australia, Ireland, Israel, the US and New Zealand, as well as India and the UK.”
label: OBJ

text: They say the rule is also incorporated in Indian domestic law, and so to ask for his release would be showing due deference to the Indian legal system.
label: SUBJ

text: No 10 braced for 'excruciating' revelations as private messages between Mandelson and ministers to be released Good morning.
label: OBJ

text: Good morning. Many people despair at the quality of governance in Britain at the moment, but in one respect we are living through a golden age; if you are interested in contemporary history, and learning about what actually happens at the heart of government, then you can now – sometimes – access the sort of information never available before.
label: SUBJ

text: Today the government is publishing a mass of information – apparently running to three volumes, and more than 1,000 pages – containing the private messages Peter Mandelson exchanged with government ministers and officials when he was ambassador to the US, and before his appointment.
label: OBJ

text: Last month a minister compared this to the evidence released as part of the Chilcot inquiry into the Iraq war.
label: OBJ

text: But the Chilcot inquiry took place in the era before WhatsApp, and it was publishing secret memos – intended for circulation within Whitehall.
label: OBJ

text: WhatsApp messages are a lot more personal; reading them is like being able to eavesdrop on a private conversation.
label: SUBJ

text: Mandelson is a man with spiky, controversial views, who loves gossip and plotting, and whose private views don’t always accord with what he has said in public.
label: SUBJ

text: It should be fascinating.
label: SUBJ

text: These documents are being published because the government has to comply with a humble address – a Commons vote mandating ministers to release information – tabled by the Conservative party.
label: OBJ

text: Several humble addresses have been passed in recent years (since this ancient parliamentary mechanism, which had been forgotten about for decades, was revived during the Brexit wars by the Labour Brexit spokesperson, a certain Keir Starmer) but none of them have been as far-reaching as this one.
label: SUBJ

text: The Conservatives tabled the humble address because they wanted to learn more about how Mandelson came to be appointed ambassador to the US despite the fact that it was known at the time that he had maintained a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein even after he was first convicted for child sex offences.
label: OBJ

text: As Kiran Stacey, Henry Dyer and Pippa Crerar report, the documents out today will imply that the Foreign Office did not seem particulary bothered about ensuring that the supposed “mitigations” in place to manage the risks associated with Mandelson being appointed amounted to very much.
label: OBJ

text: But, on the broader question of why Mandelson was appointed, we are unlikely to learn much because it is already obvious why he got the job: he wanted it, he was close to Morgan McSweeney, the PM’s chief of staff who had more influence over what Starmer did than anyone else, and McSweeney and Starmer were both persuaded that Mandelson’s fondness for dodgy billionaires would enable him to form a good relationship with Donald Trump (even though this argument was inherently flawed, because the Trump administration did not want him).
label: SUBJ

text: Instead, the main revelations this afternoon are likely to focus on what members of the government have been saying about each other in private.
label: SUBJ

text: On the Today programme Chris Mason, the BBC’s political editor, said some of the messages would be “excruciating”.
label: OBJ

text: Financial Times says: “The messages are expected to include frontbenchers and Mandelson trading humiliating remarks about Starmer.”
label: OBJ

text: Politico’s London Playbook says: “One person familiar with the content of the files told Playbook it will be ‘toe-curling’.”
label: OBJ

text: Government sources have been saying they don’t expect any of the revelations to lead to resignations.
label: OBJ

text: That’s not much of a bonus; for the Conservative party, today will probably feel like Christmas has come early.
label: SUBJ

text: Ironically, one person not likely to be embarrassed about any of this is Trump.
label: SUBJ

text: Government ministers are always diplomatic and polite about the US president in public.
label: SUBJ

text: It is fair to assume that, in private, their views are a bit more aligned with the views of normal people, like you and me.
label: SUBJ

text: But parliament agreed that material deemed “prejudicial to UK national security or international relations” would be withheld, so any juicy anti-Trump stuff will remain secret.
label: SUBJ

text: James Murray, the new health secretary, has been giving interviews this morning.
label: OBJ

text: Speaking about the release of the Mandelson files, he told Sky News:
label: OBJ

text: I think the level of transparency is going to be unprecedented.
label: SUBJ

text: The volume of information that’s going to be put out is unprecedented.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s right we do that.
label: SUBJ

text: We have been very clear that the appointment of Mandelson was wrong.
label: SUBJ

text: Parliament then decided that this information will be made public.
label: OBJ

text: The government is fully complying with that, and it’s important that we honour that commitment to transparency.
label: SUBJ

text: Here is the agenda for the day.
label: OBJ

text: 11.30am: Downing Street holds a lobby briefing.
label: OBJ

text: 2.30pm: John Healey, the defence secretary, takes questions in the Commons.
label: OBJ

text: Afternoon: The government is publishing the rest of the Mandelson files.
label: OBJ

text: Darren Jones, the chief seceretary to the PM, will make a statement to MPs to mark their publication after 3.30pm.
label: OBJ

text: House prices fell in the UK for the first time this year in May, as rising interest rates triggered by the war in Iran hurt homebuyer demand.
label: OBJ

text: The price of the average UK home dropped 0.6% in May compared with the month before, according to the lender Nationwide.
label: OBJ

text: The typical house price was 1.7% higher than the same point last year, at £278,024.
label: OBJ

text: However, that marked a slowdown from an annual growth rate of 3% in April.
label: OBJ

label: OBJ

text: The average two-year fixed rate was 5.68% at the end of May, while the average five-year fix was 5.63%, according to the financial data provider Moneyfacts.
label: OBJ

label: OBJ

label: OBJ

text: It said the impact of war in the Middle East had “fundamentally changed” its outlook for the UK property market, predicting that average house prices will fall 2% this year, compared with its previous expectation of a 2% rise.
