label: SUBJ

text: “She said that she thought he was having a ‘stroke,’ and various other really bad things, and yet never rushed onto the stage to help her troubled husband, as any good wife would do.”
label: SUBJ

text: The president followed with a truly reprehensible ego massage.
label: SUBJ

text: “The only thing she failed to mention was how well I was doing prior to his near total collapse,” he wrote.
label: OBJ

text: “In other words, as many have asked, did my strong performance in that debate cause him to plain and simple ‘choke,’ leading to his ignominious defeat, or were other reasons the cause?
label: SUBJ

text: Nobody else knows the answer to that, BUT I DO!!!”
label: SUBJ

text: Biden’s shaky performance turbocharged concerns about his age, which led him to withdraw his reelection bid and make way for Vice President Kamala Harris as the Democratic nominee.
label: SUBJ

text: A three-way tug-of-war erupted in recent months over ownership of a property on Fisher Island – one of the wealthiest zip codes in the United States – that sits in Biscayne Bay opposite the skyline of downtown Miami.
label: SUBJ

text: When TransMontaigne Partners, a Denver-based global energy company, put the parcel on the market in May 2024, interest ran high because that land represented the last remaining piece of real estate available for development on the island.
label: OBJ

text: The eventual winner of the bidding war was a Chicago-based developer called the HRP Group, which purchased the property for $180m in late September last year.
label: OBJ

text: The developer then announced ambitious plans to build condominium towers on the property at an estimated cost of $2bn.
label: OBJ

text: After a series of events that unfolded over the past eight months, most of which occurred quietly and behind closed doors, the HRP Group reportedly struck an agreement in principle to sell the property to Miami-Dade county for $400m. The developer stands to reap a windfall $220m in profit without ever breaking ground on the site – and the county’s taxpayers will have no choice but to foot the bill.
label: SUBJ

text: There was always one catch to the purchase of the property located at 1 Fisher Island Drive.
label: SUBJ

text: The site is host to a Depression-era fuel depot that services Miami’s cruise line industry, and its squat, unsightly fuel tanks have a way of ruining the otherwise spectacular views of some Fisher Island swells.
label: SUBJ

text: Those vital pieces of infrastructure would have to go – and under the terms of sale, the HRP Group agreed to demolish the fueling facility and undertake whatever environmental cleanup would be necessary before construction of the envisioned condominium towers would commence.
label: OBJ

text: As word began to spread last summer that the much-coveted property might be changing hands soon, senior officials of Miami-Dade county went into panic mode.
label: SUBJ

text: Miami is host to the busiest cruise line port in the world, and the loss of the fuel depot would deal a major blow to industry titans such as Royal Caribbean and Carnival.
label: SUBJ

text: The nearest fueling terminal is 22 miles away at Fort Lauderdale’s Port Everglades – and if several high-profile cruise companies ever decided to abandon the Port of Miami as their main point of departure in the US, it could mean the loss of hundreds of thousands of jobs for the local economy.
label: SUBJ

text: An emergency meeting of the 13-member board of the county commissioners was scheduled for 18 September to address the emerging imbroglio over the fuel depot site.
label: OBJ

text: The members who attended the meeting voted narrowly in favor of a resolution asking Daniella Levine Cava, the county mayor, to instruct the county attorney’s office to initiate legal proceedings aimed at halting the handover of the land parcel.
label: OBJ

text: The obvious option available to officials was to assert Miami-Dade county’s right to take over the fuel terminal site on the grounds of eminent domain, a legal doctrine that gives government entities the right to take over land deemed to be in the public interest in exchange for providing fair market value compensation to the property’s private owners.
label: SUBJ

text: That sent alarm bells ringing across Fisher Island, which is only accessible by ferry or helicopter to preserve its residents’ privacy and exclusivity.
label: SUBJ

text: It also mobilized the board members of the Fisher Island Community Association (Fica), Inc, a not-for-profit corporation representing more than 800 property owners, and in January this year Fica and its sister body, the Fisher Island Club (FIC), filed a lawsuit seeking to disqualify as unconstitutional any attempt by the county to seize the property on the basis of eminent domain.
label: OBJ

text: In March, the county countered with a motion to dismiss the Fica/FIC complaint.
label: OBJ

text: The legal wrangling suddenly entered a new and unexpected phase last Thursday when HRP cut a deal with the county to unload the parcel of land for a sum more than twice what it had paid less than a year ago.
label: SUBJ

text: “The only people that have been screwed here are the citizens of Miami-Dade county,” said Joe Garcia, a former congressman and prominent community leader.
label: OBJ

text: “There are only three explanations for this.
label: SUBJ

text: Massive incompetence, criminal negligence or corruption.
label: SUBJ

text: Knowing Miami-Dade county, all three are more than possible at the same time.”
label: SUBJ

text: Also crying foul were the grandees of Fica.
label: OBJ

text: The terms of HRP Group’s original purchase in September 2025 required the Chicago-based developer to hand over four acres of the land parcel to Fica.
label: OBJ

text: That sweetheart deal will be null and void if the agreement the county has apparently reached with the Chicago-based developer holds up in court.
label: SUBJ

text: Cava said through a spokesperson that she was declining to comment.
label: OBJ

text: Hydi Webb, the Port of Miami director, also declined to comment.
label: OBJ

text: Requests for an interview with the HRP Group’s chief executive officer or a spokesperson went unanswered.
label: OBJ

text: In light of that possibility, the leadership of Fica and FIC filed a lawsuit on Thursday morning that seeks to torpedo the newly concluded agreement between HRP and the county.
label: SUBJ

text: “HRP came to Fisher Island promising to clean up a century-old hazard and build a residential community,” said James L Ferraro, the Fica chairman, in a written statement that divulged the existence of the HRP-county accord.
label: OBJ

text: “Instead, they saw a pile of taxpayer cash, sold out our residents, and decided to leave a dangerous fuel farm sitting right next to our homes.”
label: SUBJ

text: As the dust settled by the end of this week, some longtime residents voiced outrage.
label: OBJ

text: “You can’t have a port without a fuel depot, and everyone involved here knew this was an indispensable asset,” noted Garcia.
label: OBJ

text: “We’re paying someone $220m for something that we couldn’t have lived without.
label: SUBJ

text: Anywhere else but Miami-Dade county, that would be a criminal act.”
label: SUBJ

text: Prices for those red orbs have soared more than any other food product over the past year to cement a spot as one of the consumer headaches du jour.
label: SUBJ

text: “The tomato has become a symbol of something much deeper,” says Isaac Bernal Carbajo, a New York City chef who lamented life’s “simplest pleasures” falling victim to price increases.
label: OBJ

text: “Something as basic as buying fresh vegetables is starting to become a serious financial decision for many families.”
label: SUBJ

text: Tomato prices are up about 40% over a year ago, according to the latest Consumer Price Index, dwarfing increases for other groceries, including coffee (up 18.5%), beef roasts (up 17.8%) and frozen fish and seafood (up 12%), among other products that have become symbols of America’s affordability squeeze.
label: SUBJ

text: A separate inflation gauge released Thursday showed that overall prices increased 3.8% in April from a year earlier, the highest reading in nearly three years.
label: OBJ

text: Alongside crop yields, experts blame price increases for tomatoes, in part, on two pillars of President Donald Trump’s second-term policies: the Iran war and tariffs.
label: OBJ

text: The war spiked gas prices and increased shipping costs.
label: OBJ

text: Meantime, the U.S. withdrew from a deal allowing duty-free imports of tomatoes from Mexico, which grows most of America’s supply.
label: OBJ

text: Usha Haley, a Wichita State University economist, says it’s “a perfect storm of trade policy, extreme weather and Mideast policy.”
label: OBJ

text: American tomato farmers cheered the withdrawal from the tomato deal last July, saying it would help rebuild their shrinking industry.
label: OBJ

text: But for consumers, it’s been painful.
label: SUBJ

text: Though the U.S. withdrew from the Mexico tomato deal in July, it took time to see the impact in the produce aisle, with more imports in late winter and early spring.
label: OBJ

text: When the tomatoes arrived, they were slapped with a 17% tariff.
label: OBJ

text: “Tariffs are undeniably a big driver of the price inflation,” says Brett Massimino, a Virginia Commonwealth University business professor.
label: OBJ

text: “Because the U.S. relies on Mexico for the majority of its tomato supply, any changes in trade policy can have a large impact.”
label: OBJ

text: U.S. tariffs collected on tomatoes ballooned from just $16,424 in 2024 to nearly $4.6 million, according to federal data, a staggering 27,879% increase.
label: SUBJ

text: As the cost trickles down, outraged shoppers have pulled out their phones in the produce aisle, shooting videos lamenting costs they said quadrupled, with some vowing to plant a garden to avoid prices of up to $8 a pound.
label: OBJ

text: But the impact has been most pronounced for businesses that rely on tomatoes as a key ingredient in their kitchens.
label: OBJ

text: MarginEdge, which tracks prices for restaurants, says grape tomatoes have increased most — 65% in just a month — but prices have gone up across all types of tomatoes.
label: OBJ

text: Phillip Coles, a professor of supply chain management at Lehigh University, says prices should drop later in the year when domestically grown tomatoes are harvested.
label: OBJ

text: Higher prices, he says, will also “induce farmers to increase planting to meet the demand, but this takes longer because of the lead time.”
label: OBJ

text: Meantime, it’s translating to a big hit for businesses like Snarf’s Sandwiches, which puts a tomato in nearly every sandwich it makes.
label: SUBJ

text: Wayne Humphrey, chief operating officer of Snarf’s, which operates dozens of stores in Colorado, Missouri and Texas, said cases of tomatoes went from costing him $27 to $93 in the space of a year, piled on top of rising expenses for other ingredients including bread and beef, as well as increased labor costs.
label: OBJ

text: “That single ingredient now costs us more than $1.7 million in additional spend annually,” says Humphrey.
label: OBJ

text: “The math is getting harder to ignore.”
label: OBJ

text: Officials in New Jersey said Monday that several protesters were arrested overnight for defying a curfew at Delaney Hall, the Newark immigration detention center that has seen more than a week of chaotic and often violent clashes.
label: OBJ

text: The state’s Democratic attorney general, Jennifer Davenport, posted on X that a group of individuals “had come to the protest armed with helmets, shields, or gas masks, [and] deliberately refused to comply with repeated orders to leave the area”, resulting in their arrest.
label: OBJ

text: [OUTLET], which had journalists at the scene, reported the number of arrests was at least between 20 and 25 – and published photographs of several people being led away in cuffs by state police.
label: OBJ

text: A social media post from advocacy groups, including the Immigration Coalition, late on Sunday said there were “over 46 Delaney Hall protesters … arrested”.
label: OBJ

text: CBS News reported that state officers in riot gear, and others on horseback, rushed the crowd less than 15 minutes after issuing a dispersal order to people gathered outside a half-mile exclusion zone around the facility, some of the scenes captured on a video posted to YouTube by bystanders.
label: OBJ

text: On Friday, Davenport and Mikie Sherrill, New Jersey’s Democratic governor, announced that state law enforcement would be taking over policing duties outside the detention center from federal Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers to try to lower tensions after eight days of confrontations with supporters of immigrants being held inside.
label: OBJ

text: Delaney Hall detainees have been engaged in hunger and labor strikes against conditions at the center, and the temporary suspension by government officials of visitation rights for their families.
label: OBJ

text: Newark’s mayor, Ras Baraka, said in a social media post on Sunday that he was mandating an immediate 9pm to 6am curfew “due to the escalating situation at Delaney Hall and the increasing need for police intervention … to protect public safety”.
label: OBJ

text: Multiple individuals, he said, were arrested on Saturday night in possession of weapons.
label: OBJ

text: In a statement on Sunday, Sherrill said violence by the protesters endangered law enforcement and the public.
label: OBJ

text: “I refuse to let these dangerous actions detract from New Jersey’s dedication to ensuring public safety, keeping people safe from ICE, and that the people detained inside Delaney Hall are treated with dignity,” she said.
label: OBJ

text: Meanwhile, the X account of the US homeland security department (DHS) posted a video on Sunday night of an apparently unarmed protester sitting on a curb who was dragged at gunpoint behind a line of officers in riot gear and shields.
label: OBJ

text: “Don’t be this guy,” the caption said.
label: OBJ

text: A succession of similar, previous DHS posts through Sunday carried messages including, “law and order”, “ZERO tolerance for rioters”, and “if you riot, you will face the consequences”.
label: OBJ

text: The DHS has claimed allegations of mistreatment or denial of medical care for detained immigrants – and complaints of meager or inedible food portions sometimes containing maggots – are “a hoax” promoted by certain politicians.
label: OBJ

text: “No lawbreakers in the history of human civilization have been better treated than illegal aliens,” the post said.
label: OBJ

text: “ALL detainees receive FULL due process and are provided comprehensive medical care and 3 meals a day.
label: OBJ

text: Our menu at Delaney Hall even includes a WIDE range of meals like fajitas, burritos, jambalaya, fruit, vegetables, salads, brownies, and cake.”
label: SUBJ

text: The government’s assertions are starkly at odds with testimony from Democratic politicians about what they saw inside the ICE facility, which is operated by the Geo Group, one of the biggest private prison companies in the US, and which has an average daily population of between 800 and 900.
label: OBJ

text: “We spoke to several individuals, none of whom has a criminal record, many of whom have been detained here at Delaney Hall for months,” top US House Democrat Hakeem Jeffries told reporters after a congressional oversight visit Sunday with party congressional colleagues Josh Gottheimer, Rob Menendez Jr and LaMonica McIver.
label: OBJ

text: “The lack of access to quality food, that’s not America.
label: SUBJ

text: The lack of access to adequate medical treatment, that’s not America.
label: SUBJ

text: The retaliation that’s taking place, that’s not America.
label: SUBJ

text: “The fact there are 18-year-old high school girls being held here is not America.”
label: SUBJ

text: Jeffries said the Republican Trump administration would “be held accountable” for what he and his colleagues saw.
label: OBJ

text: “Delaney Hall should be shut down, and every single individual – particularly those at a high level connected to this facility – they’re engaging in a depraved indifference to human life,” he said.
label: OBJ

text: The DHS, in a direct response to Jeffries on Sunday afternoon, said: “This is a detention center – we do not provide luxury accommodations.
label: OBJ

text: What we do provide are basic necessities.”
label: OBJ

text: Sherrill, who has been criticized by protesters for her response to the situation, confirmed on Sunday that family visitation at Delaney Hall had been restored to at least part of the site, and would resume fully on Monday after it was suspended by DHS citing the violence in the facility’s proximity.
label: OBJ

text: Tech billionaires have shelled out hundreds of millions of dollars ahead of the 2 June primary election in California, in an unrivaled attempt to influence who gets to run the state that Silicon Valley calls home.
label: SUBJ

text: The industry has used a cover-all-bases approach, funding candidates and ballot measures big and small, contributing to what looks to be the most expensive primary season in California history.
label: SUBJ

text: The goal, experts say, is to gain both political and regulatory leverage that will perpetuate dominance in business.
label: OBJ

text: “This money is flowing in the direction of politicians that can be influential in defining the regulatory agenda for the next five years,” said Francesco Trebbi, a public policy professor at the University of California in Berkeley.
label: OBJ

text: “Reinforcing the cycle of economic power produces political power, and political power further establishes economic power.
label: OBJ

text: So, this cycle is ongoing.”
label: OBJ

text: Combing through campaign finance filings with California’s secretary of state, [OUTLET] found:
label: OBJ

text: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has spent $66m since January, more than any other donor, to fight a billionaire tax that’s up for a vote on the November ballot.
label: OBJ

text: Democratic gubernatorial candidate Matt Mahan has received more donations than any other candidate, including from top executives at Google, Amazon, Snap, LinkedIn, Reddit and Palantir.
label: OBJ

text: Crypto mogul Chris Larsen has funded three Super Pacs with $26m to sway campaigns across California, including giving $1m to back a primary candidate for state insurance commissioner.
label: OBJ

text: Google and Meta have collectively funded a Super Pac with $10m to back assembly and senate candidates in local district races across the state.
label: OBJ

text: Silicon Valley money is flowing toward city primaries as well as state-level ones, with tech-backed Pacs sponsoring voter guides suggesting how to vote on local tax measures.
label: OBJ

text: For Silicon Valley, pouring money into politics at this moment is existential as it races to develop artificial intelligence.
label: SUBJ

text: With favorable candidates in office, tech companies say they will be able to grow at a breakneck rate while avoiding stifling regulations.
label: OBJ

text: The vast amount of spending that’s been disclosed in public records likely isn’t even the half of it, Trebbi said.
label: OBJ

text: People looking to sway election outcomes often fund dark money entities that aren’t traceable through campaign finance filings.
label: OBJ

text: “These people are sophisticated political givers, so they will use both visible and invisible forms of influence,” Trebbi said.
label: OBJ

text: What we’re seeing now is “just the tip of the iceberg”. Money-for-influence leaderboard
label: SUBJ

text: Money-for-influence leaderboard The influx of dollars has meant that voters from Oakland to Bakersfield to Orange County have been bombarded with TV ads, robotexts and mailers touting various issues and candidates sponsored by super political action committees (Pacs) funded by the tech industry.
label: SUBJ

text: Top spenders for these Super Pacs include billionaires Larsen and Brin.
label: OBJ

text: Larsen, the co-founder of crypto company Ripple Labs, is worth about $12bn and has spent millions on more than a dozen primary campaigns up and down the state, targeting races and issues at a city and county level, as well as bigger state-level races.
label: OBJ

text: Brin, worth about $290bn, has homed in on fighting a one-time 5% tax on the state’s billionaires up for a vote in November, the proceeds from which are intended to help cover education, food assistance and healthcare programs.
label: OBJ

text: To date, Brin has donated at least $66m to a Super Pac dedicated to blocking the billionaire tax, according to campaign finance filings with the state.
label: OBJ

text: The former Alphabet president also spent $500,000 in San Francisco last month to battle a city measure that seeks to expand a tax on high-paid CEOs, which is up for a vote on 2 June.
label: OBJ

text: These donations come even as Brin moved out of California late last year to Nevada.
label: OBJ

text: Larsen and Brin did not respond to requests for comment.
label: OBJ

text: Along with contributing millions to Super Pacs and candidates in California, the tech world is also spending eye-popping amounts on lobbying.
label: SUBJ

text: An analysis by news site CalMatters found that in 2025 alone, the tech industry paid $39m to lobby the state government.
label: OBJ

text: That’s more than any year prior and surpasses what was spent by the oil and gas industry, which typically tops the high roller list.
label: OBJ

text: According to a Bloomberg analysis, the biggest tech and AI companies spent a collective $109m on federal lobbying in 2025; that their state lobbying in California is equivalent to 36% of their federal spend showcases the state’s importance to the tech industry.
label: OBJ

text: Tech picks a favorite in the governor’s race
label: SUBJ

text: Of the 62 candidates listed on the 2 June primary ballot, one has stood out as the tech industry’s darling: Matt Mahan.
label: SUBJ

text: The centrist Democrat and upstart mayor from San Jose, a large city in Silicon Valley, entered the race late and quickly made headlines as he racked up contributions from a who’s who of the tech industry.
label: OBJ

text: Before Mahan got involved in politics in 2020, he had a career in the tech sector.
label: OBJ

text: He was an undergraduate at Harvard with Facebook co-founder Mark Zuckerberg, and in 2014 co-founded a startup with funding from the Salesforce CEO, Marc Benioff, tech investor Ron Conway and Napster co-founder Sean Parker.
label: OBJ

text: Since Mahan’s candidacy announcement in late January, he’s received nearly $50m in contributions, according to Politico – more than any other gubernatorial candidate (with the exception of Tom Steyer’s self-funded campaign of about $200m).
label: OBJ

text: Mahan has received donations from prominent venture capitalists, along with former and current executives from Google, Amazon, Snap, eBay, PayPal, Stripe, LinkedIn, DoorDash, Reddit, Netflix, Palantir, Anduril, Roblox, Riot Games and more, public records show.
label: OBJ

text: Google’s Brin donated the maximum limit for an individual campaign donation at $78,400 and contributed $1m to the pro-Mahan Super Pac Deliver for California, according to public records.
label: OBJ

text: Mahan flew to Lake Tahoe where Brin lives in March to make a personal appeal to the billionaire and his conservative influencer girlfriend, the New York Times reported.
label: OBJ

text: Brin’s girlfriend alleges that Mahan texted Brin afterwards to apologize for attending a No Kings rally.
label: OBJ

text: Mahan’s overtures to both progressives and conservatives haven’t won him many friends among the state’s leading Democrats.
label: OBJ

text: Silicon Valley congressman Ro Khanna chose Steyer to endorse and state assembly members from Mahan’s district have publicly criticized him, saying he was “handpicked” by the tech industry.
label: OBJ

text: Similarly, Lorena Gonzalez Fletcher, president of the powerful California Labor Federation, said Mahan is the only democrat she’s not promoting because she’s “opposed to the candidate funded by Trump’s big tech billionaires”.
label: OBJ

text: Mahan said he has no plans to cater to special interests and his goal is for the system to work for everyone.
label: OBJ

text: “I’m not running for tech, and if you look at my record – I’ve been in public office now for six years – I think you’d be hard pressed to find – you would not find a single example of me ever doing something to benefit the industry to the detriment of the community,” Mahan said.
label: OBJ

text: “If anything, I’ve fought hard to get them to do their fair share.”
label: OBJ

text: The influx of tech cash into Mahan’s race hasn’t bolstered it as much as early predictions forecast.
label: SUBJ

text: His campaign has failed to gain traction with a wider audience and polls have put him at just 4% of the vote.
label: SUBJ

text: The Brin-funded Deliver for California Super Pac shuttered last month.
label: OBJ

text: Mahan did not respond to further questions about his interactions with Brin or the termination of the Super Pac.
label: OBJ

text: Targeting state and local primaries
label: OBJ

text: Although the tech industry has mostly focused on a sole candidate for the governor’s race, it has taken more of a scattershot approach in local campaigns.
label: OBJ

text: Silicon Valley money has infiltrated nearly every segment of politics – from local ballot measures to state congressional campaigns to the race for California’s new insurance commissioner.
label: SUBJ

text: The tech executive who appears most dedicated to local politics is Larsen, the crypto mogul.
label: SUBJ

text: He’s funded Super Pacs aimed at different causes and candidates.
label: OBJ

text: The Golden State Promise Super Pac has received a total of $10m entirely from Larsen and Ripple Labs, public records show.
label: OBJ

text: The Pac, which is devoted to combating the billionaire tax that’s up for a vote in November, launched an attack ad against the tax earlier this month.
label: OBJ

text: Another Super Pac supported by Larsen is geared toward the state’s insurance commissioner race.
label: OBJ

text: Earlier this month, Larsen donated more than $1m to the Pac, Californians for an Affordable Future, which is dedicated to electing Ben Allen, a democrat.
label: OBJ

text: It’s a heated primary race with several candidates vying for the seat, including Bernie Sanders-backed Jane Kim, also a democrat.
label: OBJ

text: Larsen has spread his money across elections for California’s state legislature too, mostly through a Super Pac called Grow California.
label: OBJ

text: He’s donated $15m to the Pac, while crypto evangelist Tim Draper has contributed $5m, according to public records.
label: OBJ

text: Grow California’s stated goal is to “rebuild a state capital”.
label: OBJ

text: The Super Pac has injected hundreds of thousands of dollars into roughly a dozen state assembly and senate primaries across California.
label: OBJ

text: For example, Mark Pulido, who’s running for assembly in Orange county, has received more than $1.5m from Grow California.
label: OBJ

text: Likewise, a senate candidate in Northern California’s Alameda County, Scott Sakakihara, has received more than $500,000 from the Pac.
label: OBJ

text: “We have a group of people who are not acting in a pragmatic way.
label: OBJ

text: They’re not looking for balance.
label: SUBJ

text: They’re completely fucking owned by one side,” Larsen told Politico, in reference to organized labor’s power in the legislature.
label: OBJ

text: “So we’re going to work on taking out those people who are not working for the people of California.”
label: OBJ

text: Google and Meta have supported a similar Super Pac, California Leads, with $5m each and have distributed funds to several candidates in the Central Valley, as well as to many of the same contenders as Grow California.
label: OBJ

text: According to public records, Pulido has received nearly $750,000 from California Leads.
label: OBJ

text: The Super Pac’s stated mission is “supporting leaders focused on California’s future”.
label: OBJ

text: John Bennett, director of the advocacy organization California Initiative for Technology and Democracy, said spending upwards of $500,000 on a local district primary is a “huge sum of money”.
label: OBJ

text: He’s been studying the races and said the bulk of tech spending has gone to about a dozen open seats in the state legislature.
label: OBJ

text: “They’ve been hyper-focused on those open seats, not going after incumbents this time around,” Bennett said.
label: OBJ

text: “So, it seems like they’re doing a long-term strategy to slowly turn the legislature to become more friendly to them.”
label: SUBJ

text: Other companies, like Airbnb and Uber, have also donated to local assembly and senate races across the state but with smaller contributions.
label: OBJ

text: City campaigns are seeing a tech infusion too.
label: OBJ

text: Joe Lonsdale, a Palantir co-founder, contributed to former reality TV star and Los Angeles mayor hopeful Spencer Pratt – even though Lonsdale lives in Texas, records show.
label: OBJ

text: And several 501(c)(4) groups backed by Silicon Valley money have cropped up across the Bay Area sending out mailers and robotexts with voter guides that highlight preferred local candidates, along with suggestions to vote down issues like a union-backed parcel tax.
label: OBJ

text: “Now they’re going at this from multiple fronts,” Bennett said.
label: OBJ

text: “They’re spending in elections, they’re spending in the legislature, and they’re trying to do whatever they can to ensure that they don’t lose their foothold in this economic system.”
label: OBJ

text: Lauren Gambino contributed reporting
label: OBJ

text: Downtown Los Angeles’s fashion district, the largest apparel manufacturing hub in the United States, is a neighborhood in freefall.
label: SUBJ

text: While 83% of clothing cut and sewn in the United States is made here, the district has suffered in recent years as visitation and sales have plummeted.
label: OBJ

text: “I went from making $2,000 a day to making now $500, sometimes $700,” said Fernando Carmona, who owns the women’s dress store AP Design by Rocca.
label: OBJ

text: He added that rent for his store was $8,250 a month.
label: OBJ

text: On a recent Tuesday afternoon, Carmona was hustling.
label: OBJ

text: Glittering dresses in red, blue and black lined the walls from ceiling to floor.
label: OBJ

text: On the street, foot traffic was a slow trickle.
label: OBJ

text: These days, Carmona said he can’t slack off for even a moment, or he might lose precious business.
label: OBJ

text: Fernando’s shop is located in the 107-block neighborhood made up of storefronts, showrooms and factories, and is known for its local businesses that sell everything from tailored tuxedos to flower bouquets at cheap prices.
label: OBJ

text: Carmona, who opened his store in another location before the pandemic, said foot traffic had never been as bad as it is now.
label: OBJ

text: Immigration enforcement raids ordered by the Trump administration last summer have left a scar on workers across the district, who are afraid to go to work.
label: SUBJ

text: And, he said, sales haven’t bounced back to their pre-pandemic levels.
label: OBJ

text: But there’s a glimmer of hope on the horizon.
label: SUBJ

text: In two years, LA will host the Olympic Games, and leaders are hopeful the global event will be a boon for the city.
label: OBJ

text: The Games are expected to bring millions of visitors and generate billions of dollars in income to a city still reeling from a year of turbulent events including last year’s wildfires.
label: OBJ

text: Leaders across the fashion district, as well as LA’s broader fashion industry, have hopes that the Games can bring a life-saving shock to the area.
label: OBJ

text: “The Olympics has been the light at the end of the tunnel,” said Anthony Rodriguez, CEO and president of the LA Fashion District, a body that works with local governments to manage the neighborhood. An Olympic ‘jump-start’
label: OBJ

text: An Olympic ‘jump-start’ In the run-up to the Games, every host city is required to put on a “cultural Olympiad”, a suite of events and activities that highlight the cultural richness of the host city.
label: OBJ

text: LA is no different – but it is behind.
label: SUBJ

text: LA28 delivered its cultural plan six months past its self-imposed deadline, with few details about what events it plans to produce.
label: OBJ

text: The department of cultural affairs, a city department that is facilitating cultural planning between the city and LA28, released a shortlist of neighborhoods it plans to designate as “cultural hubs” places that will probably be funded to host cultural activities and events.
label: OBJ

text: The fashion district isn’t on the list, but Rodriguez thinks an official cultural hub designation would help “jump-start” his neighborhood.
label: OBJ

text: He said he wanted the Olympics to be an opportunity for Angelenos to rediscover how cool the fashion district is.
label: OBJ

text: “We’re in this weird place right now,” he said.
label: OBJ

text: “There’s so many things working against us, and by the time we figure one of those out
label: OBJ

text: and how to navigate past that, that progress is erased because we got three other things lining up to set us back.”
label: OBJ

text: LA hosted the Olympics in 1984, and its legacy is still felt today.
label: OBJ

text: During the Olympic arts festival that preceded those Games, a series of opera performances were so successful that they inspired a group to establish the LA Opera, among the top 10 largest opera houses in the United States.
label: OBJ

text: After the Games ended, the city was left with a surplus large enough to fund an organization that to this day supports youth sports across the region.
label: OBJ

text: Neighborhoods are already gearing up for an influx of visitors and their dollars.
label: OBJ

text: Downtown Los Angeles’s convention center, which will host fencing and wrestling, is undergoing a $2.62bn renovation.
label: OBJ

text: Earlier this year, the city approved the creation of a special arts district that will offer entertainment packages for people visiting from out of town.
label: OBJ

text: Nick Griffin, the executive vice-president of the DTLA Alliance, a consortium of business owners in downtown, said the Games will bring unparalleled attention to downtown in the form of visitors and viewership.
label: OBJ

text: “The economic opportunity that that represents is incalculable.
label: SUBJ

text: You cannot buy that kind of advertising,” he said.
label: OBJ

text: Rodriguez isn’t the only one who wants to ensure that the Olympics are a major economic driver to the fashion district.
label: SUBJ

text: Jonathan Saven, a fashion industry veteran, has ambitious plans for the Olympics.
label: OBJ

text: He recently presented a sprawling proposal to LA28 that includes multiple events across the city and features LA as a “global atelier”.
label: OBJ

text: Saven, the CEO of LA-based women’s wear brand L’Agence, said he had already had conversations with leaders at YouTube about the possibility of livestreaming parts of it.
label: OBJ

text: Saven is also the co-founder of the Denim Institute, a museum and denim school opening in the fashion district next year.
label: OBJ

text: Through the school, he and his co-founder, Loren Cronk, want to bring students from other countries to LA to learn about local manufacturing.
label: OBJ

text: Ultimately, Saven said LA28 told him they would not officially sanction his proposal, but he’s hoping to galvanize LA’s fashion community to produce something that highlights the brands that are still manufacturing locally.
label: OBJ

text: LA28 declined to be interviewed for this story.
label: OBJ

text: “It’s shocking for people to learn that so many of the garments that we buy at different retailers are still produced in the United States and being produced in Los Angeles,” said Daisy Gonzalez, who is the campaigns director at the Garment Workers Center, an organization that advocates for more than 40,000 garment workers across LA.
label: OBJ

text: Offshoring production to foreign countries, wage theft and recent immigration enforcement raids have led to a decline in workers.
label: OBJ

text: Gonzalez said the Olympics should be an opportunity to make event merchandise here, which could lead to much-needed jobs.
label: OBJ

text: Rodriguez said the district had historically been a key economic driver for the city.
label: OBJ

text: And he wants to keep it that way.
label: OBJ

text: “This isn’t something that I want just for the fashion district.
label: OBJ

text: This is something that I want for all of downtown,” he said.
label: OBJ

text: “This is the last corner of LA where people can live out their dreams.”
label: OBJ

text: Claude Lemieux’s brain is being donated to the Boston University CTE Center to research the long-term effects of repetitive brain injuries, his family said Saturday in a statement released by daughter Claudia Lemieux Bishop.
label: OBJ

text: Lemieux died of suicide at age 60 on Thursday, according to authorities, after earlier in the week serving as the Montreal Canadiens’ torchbearer prior to a playoff game.
label: OBJ

text: He played nearly 1,500 NHL games with six teams from 1983-2009 and was known for his hard-hitting style and ability to perform in big games on the way to winning the Stanley Cup four times.
label: OBJ

text: The family said it gave the CTE Center permission to publicly share any findings with Lemieux’s name, adding that no conclusions should be drawn regarding any diagnosis.
label: OBJ

text: “Claude dedicated his post-playing career to helping the next generation,” the family said, referring to Lemieux becoming an agent.
label: OBJ

text: “By allowing his name to be connected to this research, we hope his life can contribute to greater understanding, more honest conversations and better protection for athletes and families in the years ahead.”
label: OBJ

text: “I’m so sorry to disappoint the folks who have purchased tickets to my shows, but I have decided to take the rest of the year off from touring to focus on my health,” the 92-year-old singer wrote on social media Friday.
label: OBJ

text: Valli, the frontman and only original member still performing with the Four Seasons, launched an extended farewell tour billed “The Last Encores” in October 2023.
label: OBJ

text: Shows scheduled for April of this year were rescheduled to dates in summer and fall.
label: OBJ

text: Now, all eight performances remaining for 2026 in cities from Scottsdale, Arizona, to Detroit have been called off entirely.
label: OBJ

text: The Four Seasons found fame in the 1960s, but the band’s hits –- from “Sherry” to “December 1963 (Oh What a Night)” –- span decades.
label: OBJ

text: The Four Seasons sold 100 million records worldwide and were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1990.
label: OBJ

text: Valli, who also established a successful solo career, received a Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award in 2026.
label: OBJ

text: Known for keeping a rigorous touring schedule even as he has aged, Valli appeared to leave the door open for future performances.
label: OBJ

text: “I’m looking forward to getting healthy and seeing you all again soon,” Valli concluded his Friday message.
label: OBJ

text: With the Fifa World Cup just two weeks away, immigrant rights advocates in the 11 US host cities are mobilizing to protect fans and residents from immigration enforcement activities this summer.
label: OBJ

text: In Los Angeles, a labor union representing more than 2,000 hospitality workers at SoFi Stadium is threatening to strike if agents do not stay away from the venue, which is expected to draw about 70,000 fans per match.
label: OBJ

text: In Dallas, the civil rights group El Movimiento DFW is handing out hundreds of whistle kits – including information on how to obtain a free consultation with an immigration attorney – at churches, businesses and apartment complexes in case US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents begin detaining people near matches.
label: OBJ

text: More than 120 civil society groups have even issued a travel warning to the 10 million potential visitors about “serious rights violations” under the current political climate, including “arbitrary denial of entry and risk of arrest, detention and/or deportation”.
label: OBJ

text: The world’s largest sporting event, which kicks off on 11 June, arrives at a cataclysmic moment in the US: amid Donald Trump’s large-scale, aggressive immigration crackdown, at least 18 people have died this year in ICE custody and multiple US citizens have been killed.
label: SUBJ

text: Immigration activists say they have received conflicting reports about the presence and role of ICE agents at the World Cup: Trump administration officials have not ruled out the possibility of arrests near the games, despite assurances from the secretary of state, Marco Rubio, that ICE would not operate inside stadiums.
label: OBJ

text: “The Department of Homeland Security is working around the clock with federal, state, local and international partners to ensure a safe and secure environment for players, fans and communities hosting these events,” said Lauren Bis, the Department of Homeland Security acting assistant secretary.
label: OBJ

text: “The safety and security of the American people and the millions of visitors attending these events remain our highest priority.”
label: OBJ

text: In response, organizers are staying vigilant on multiple fronts, bolstering rapid response networks and legal aid resources.
label: OBJ

text: “We know that soccer is something many in the Latino immigrant community grew up playing or watching,” said Christine Bolaños, communications director at Workers Defense Action Fund, one of the signatories of a related advisory in Texas.
label: OBJ

text: “These games are meant to bring people together, and we want fans to know the risks and be prepared for encounters.”
label: OBJ

text: Preparing to rapidly respond to ICE
label: OBJ

text: Many of the grassroots efforts leading up to the event focus on responding to ICE agent raids, arrests and interactions in real time.
label: OBJ

text: After sustained pressure, police in Atlanta, Seattle and Los Angeles have announced they will not be cooperating with ICE in enforcement matters.
label: OBJ

text: Several Democratic lawmakers have introduced bills prohibiting ICE activity near matches – but they are stalled in the GOP-controlled House.
label: OBJ

text: “When there’s a tenfold increase in the security apparatus, there’s a real risk that local residents – immigrants and non-immigrants – will be caught in the law enforcement dragnet,” said Jennifer Li, a leader of the national coalition Dignity 26.
label: OBJ

text: In response, Li said she had been connecting experienced immigration attorneys with rapid response groups in host cities.
label: OBJ

text: With the World Cup approaching, she is working with the legal team behind Airport Lawyers to reboot a website created during the first Trump term to connect volunteer attorneys with foreign nationals affected by the travel ban.
label: OBJ

text: Separately, Li said she was coordinating with the Independent Supporters Council to launch a fan embassy and resource portal with workers’ rights information and immigration hotlines for fans and residents in host cities.
label: OBJ

text: In Miami, which leads the country in immigration arrests, advocates are tapping into a rapid response network that activists say has been especially active over the past year, as local police increased collaboration with ICE.
label: OBJ

text: Ahead of the World Cup, organizers have been urging immigrant communities to report potential ICE activities to a hotline operated by the Florida Rapid Response Alliance for Immigrant Safety and Empowerment, a cohort of immigrant rights groups.
label: OBJ

text: The alliance also has a team of legal observers who are trained to document ICE presence.
label: OBJ

text: “For us, those are two lifelines,” said Yareliz Mendez-Zamora, an organizer with the American Friends Service, one of the coalition partners.
label: OBJ

text: “These are scary times, but also beautiful times when we’re able to work and protect each other.”
label: OBJ

text: Creating safe zones in host cities
label: OBJ

text: For some immigrant and workers’ organizers in Seattle, which is expecting more than 750,000 visitors for the World Cup, the international spotlight is a chance to push their demands further.
label: OBJ

text: CJ Garcia, the co-director of organizing at Working Washington, said she was worried that the Trump administration could use the event as a staging ground for ICE to “make an example of Seattle and other cities that have a history of supporting immigrants”.
label: OBJ

text: The global stage, she said, “is an opportunity to announce our expectations as a society”.
label: OBJ

text: Garcia said her organization canvassed more than 150 restaurants in Capitol Hill, which was just a few miles from where the games will be played, over the past couple of months, talking to owners and workers directly about what issues they anticipated and how they could support one another.
label: OBJ

text: In response to their concerns, the group has been providing fourth amendment training to service workers, covering their rights in public and private spaces, communication strategies in case of a crisis or emergency and best practices for how to respond should ICE try to detain a co-worker.
label: OBJ

text: Working Washington is among dozens of non-profits, faith leaders and labor unions that joined the national “No ICE in the Cup” campaign earlier this month, which organizes ICE-free watch parties and youth soccer tournaments in all the host cities.
label: OBJ

text: Working Washington is co-hosting a kick-off party in Seattle on 11 June.
label: OBJ

text: Another group involved in the campaign is West Philadelphia Corridor Collaborative, one of the city’s largest business associations.
label: OBJ

text: In late May, the organization launched a two-week canvassing drive to collect signatures from businesses – primarily restaurants, bars and nightlife establishments – pledging that they would serve as safe spaces for immigrants and provide critical resources about ICE enforcement.
label: OBJ

text: The goal is to bring at least 65 businesses onboard, said its president, Jabari Jones.
label: OBJ

text: The group is also building a rapid response network of immigration attorneys who can be promptly deployed to businesses that get raided by ICE.
label: OBJ

text: For the business community, he said, limiting ICE presence at the World Cup is more an economic development issue than a political or ideological issue.
label: OBJ

text: A family of four from Massachusetts who were killed when a bus crashed into multiple vehicles in Virginia were traveling to a wedding with a carload of homemade desserts for the celebration.
label: OBJ

text: The family wedding will go forward Sunday in South Carolina, but it also will be a time to mourn the loss of Dmitri and Ecaterina Doncev and their two children, Emily and Mark, a relative said Saturday.
label: OBJ

text: “A son, a father — the whole family — everyone that has been dear to us,” Carolina Bublik said.
label: OBJ

text: The Doncevs were killed when a motorcoach caused a chain-reaction crash with vehicles that had slowed down for a work zone on Interstate 95 in Stafford County around 2:35 a.m. Friday, authorities said.
label: OBJ

text: The bus struck a Suburban, which then hit an Acura carrying the Doncev family, police said.
label: OBJ

text: Priscilla Mafalda, 25, of Worcester, Massachusetts, was in the Chevrolet SUV and also died.
label: OBJ

text: More people were treated for injuries, including one who was in critical condition, though most were discharged, Mary Washington Healthcare said.
label: OBJ

text: The bus driver, Jing S. Dong, 48, of Staten Island, New York, was charged with two counts of involuntary manslaughter, and additional charges were likely, Virginia State Police said.
label: OBJ

text: The prosecutor’s office in Stafford County said Dong was arrested and would be in custody while he is treated for his injuries at a hospital.
label: OBJ

text: Prosecutors said in a statement that Dong’s first court appearance will not be scheduled until he is discharged but a magistrate approved holding him without bond until that time.
label: OBJ

text: It also said prosecutors saw enough probable cause to believe Dong was “driving in a criminally negligent manner.”
label: OBJ

text: It was unclear whether Dong has an attorney who could speak on his behalf.
label: OBJ

text: The case did not yet appear in the state’s online court records, and a call to the area’s public defender went unanswered at a closed office Saturday.
label: OBJ

text: The National Transportation Safety Board is investigating the crash, a monthslong task separate from the work of state police.
label: OBJ

text: NTSB board member Tom Chapman revealed few new details but said the bus was moving at a high rate of speed.
label: OBJ

text: “It seems fairly clear that if there was any braking there wasn’t much, because of the speed and severity of the collision,” Chapman said.
label: OBJ

text: The bus, which was taking people from New York to North Carolina, was operated by E&P Travel Inc., based in Kings Mountain, North Carolina.
label: OBJ

text: Chapman said the driver’s language proficiency would be part of the NTSB investigation.
label: OBJ

text: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy, citing police, said on social media that Dong, a native of China, does not speak English.
label: OBJ

text: Dmitri Doncev, 45, was a nurse who worked at Holyoke Medical Center.
label: OBJ

text: Ecaterina Doncev, 44, was a hairstylist who spent days making desserts for the family wedding, Bublik said.
label: OBJ

text: They emigrated to the U.S. from Moldova in 2008 and settled in Greenfield, Massachusetts, she said.
label: OBJ

text: Dmitri and brother Iuri tried to stay together while traveling in separate vehicles to South Carolina.
label: OBJ

text: “At some point they ended up getting separated,” Bublik said.
label: OBJ

text: “Dmitri said, ‘You go ahead.
label: OBJ

text: I’ll catch up later.’
label: OBJ

text: It was a big shock when Iuri arrived at the house.
label: OBJ

text: Dmitri should have arrived around the same time.
label: SUBJ

text: When his car did not show up, and he wasn’t picking up the phone — that’s when the family started panicking.”
label: OBJ

text: Good morning. It has been two weeks since details of a settlement in the case of Trump v the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) first emerged.
label: OBJ

text: An out-of-court agreement with the US president created a $1.8bn fund for the Trump administration to dish out at its discretion.
label: OBJ

text: In response, the outrage has been unrelenting.
label: OBJ

text: Critics argue the result stinks of cronyism and corruption, effectively a “scheme for the Trumps to reward political friends while indirectly benefiting the family”.
label: OBJ

text: There has been rare pushback from within Trump’s own party: more than a dozen Republican senators have reportedly urged the administration to change course.
label: OBJ

text: YouGov polling found a majority of Democrats and Republicans oppose the fund.
label: OBJ

text: On Friday, a federal judge reopened the case, after a bipartisan group of federal judges filed a lawsuit in Florida arguing the settlement “is a product of collusion and is itself a fraud on the court”.
label: OBJ

text: For today’s First Edition, I spoke to [OUTLET] US political enterprise editor, George Zornick.
label: OBJ

text: We discussed the background to this case, Trump’s term of self-enrichment and how his (relatively) new team is covering Trump’s second presidency. First, the headlines. Five big stories
label: SUBJ

text: First, the headlines. Five big stories UK politics | A trove of government documents about Peter Mandelson contains no record of any measures taken to mitigate serious security concerns over his appointment as Washington ambassador, [OUTLET] has learned.
label: OBJ

text: Health news | A daily pill can double survival time in patients with the world’s deadliest cancer, according to the results of a clinical trial that experts are saying is a “gamechanger” and one of the biggest breakthroughs in decades.
label: OBJ

text: Lebanon | European leaders have condemned Israel’s expanding incursion into Lebanon, after its military captured the medieval Beaufort castle and Benjamin Netanyahu vowed to push even deeper into the country.
label: OBJ

text: Employment | An Indian citizen who came to the UK to work as a care worker through the post-Brexit visa scheme has been awarded nearly £30,000, because his employer failed to give him a single day of work for a year.
label: OBJ

text: UK news | Facebook whistleblower Sarah Wynn-Williams was forced to sit in silence on stage at an event at Hay festival, after lawyers advised her not to speak because of ongoing legal action brought by Meta.
label: OBJ

text: In depth: ‘The level of graft and self-enrichment is truly unprecedented’
label: SUBJ

text: The settlement is a response to a federal case dating to January 2024, when an IRS contractor was sentenced to five years in prison for leaking the tax returns of high-net worth individuals.
label: OBJ

text: These revealed Trump paid just $750 in federal income tax in 2016 and 2017, and no federal income taxes in 10 of the previous 15 years.
label: OBJ

text: Trump sued the IRS for $10bn over the leak, explains George.
label: OBJ

text: It seems to be a habit of his.
label: SUBJ

text: A federal judge ordered a hearing to determine whether a president could sue federal agencies he oversees.
label: OBJ

text: “The IRS wrote an internal memo saying the lawsuit is weak and should be fought …
label: OBJ

text: Somewhere along the way, the administration overruled that position and settled.”
label: SUBJ

text: An agreement was announced: in exchange for dropping the lawsuit, US authorities would be “forever barred” from auditing the past tax returns of Trump and his family.
label: OBJ

text: “It also created a $1.8bn fund to provide financial restitution to people subject to ‘weaponised lawfare’ from the federal government,” says George.
label: OBJ

text: This has been widely criticised as a slush fund of taxpayer cash to benefit Trump allies.
label: OBJ

text: Acting US attorney general, Todd Blanche, suggests anyone, not just Trump’s associates, can apply for financial restitution from the fund – although ultimately a five-member commission appointed by the attorney general, not a court or a jury, will get to decide on the merit of those applications – a highly unusual way of administering publicly funded compensation schemes.
label: SUBJ

text: Of the five commissioners appointed to oversee payouts, four are to be selected by Blanche himself, Trump’s personal lawyer turned legal enforcer.
label: SUBJ

text: The fifth will be appointed in consultation with congressional leadership.
label: OBJ

text: “These commissioners can be fired at any time, and for any reason, by Trump himself.
label: OBJ

text: The idea that this is a non-partisan fund?
label: SUBJ

text: I am sceptical,” says George.
label: OBJ

text: It’s down to the sheer scale of stories like this emanating from the United States that [OUTLET] US’s political enterprise team was assembled.
label: SUBJ

text: “Every day here … [there are] constant curve balls.
label: OBJ

text: It puts such pressure on the bandwidth of reporters and the attention span of readers,” says George.
label: OBJ

text: But with people specifically tasked with examining abuses of power at the federal level, George’s team are able to keep laser-focused.
label: SUBJ

text: “We have the flexibility, once our teeth are in a story, to stay there.”
label: OBJ

text: The White House is occupying much of their attention.
label: OBJ

text: “In my lifetime, and the modern era,” George tells me, “the level of graft and self-enrichment we are witnessing is truly unprecedented.
label: OBJ

text: I say this in a journalistically neutral way: there is no modern parallel.”
label: SUBJ

text: In 2016, in advance of his first presidency, Trump signalled he’d cede day-to-day control of his businesses to avoid conflicts of interest.
label: OBJ

text: Despite a 2017 announcement that he was handing control of his empire to his sons, Donald Jr and Eric, it soon transpired the fledgling president retained direct ties to his commercial ventures.
label: OBJ

text: “His message then was, ‘I am stepping back from all of this’,” says George. “It wasn’t believable.”
label: OBJ

text: “It wasn’t believable.” Trump has lamented the lack of deals he made during his first tenure.
label: OBJ

text: This time, there’s no such pretension: “I found out that nobody cared.
label: SUBJ

text: I’m allowed to,” he told the New York Times.
label: OBJ

text: In January, the New Yorker suggested that Trump and his family have racked up profits in the region of $4bn by leveraging his presidential position.
label: OBJ

text: The Trump family has been busy. “[Trump’s sons]
label: OBJ

text: “[Trump’s sons] Eric and Don Jr, and [son-in-law] Jared Kushner are conducting deals in places that are key to US foreign policy, building towers, golf courses and resorts.”
label: OBJ

text: None of this is likely a coincidence, George believes.
label: OBJ

text: “Foreign governments, as part of their strategy to deal with Trump, are green-lighting projects where you wonder: would that really have been allowed otherwise?”
label: SUBJ

text: The domestic market is proving just as lucrative.
label: OBJ

text: “The sons have been particularly fast to spin up companies – whether drones, AI, crypto – which have been pretty good at winning big government contracts.”
label: OBJ

text: Last week, ProPublica reported that the White House asked the Pentagon to give a $620m loan to a company with ties to Trump’s eldest son.
label: OBJ

text: The Trump family cryptocurrency firm, meanwhile, has seen their wealth balloon by billions.
label: SUBJ

text: A minority stake is owned by the UAE: its $500m investment steered $187m to Trump family entities.
label: OBJ

text: Allegations of self-enrichment from Trump’s first term focused on Trump’s Mar-a-Lago resort.
label: OBJ

text: “People would spend money eating, staying renting the ballroom, hopefully on a weekend the president was there” – all of which, critics argued, was a way to keep a steady stream of income flowing towards the president’s businesses.
label: OBJ

text: Since taking office again, this has continued, “but in the second term, it’s a lot more out in the open”, says George, adding that he believes it’s been “turbo-charged”.
label: OBJ

text: A 2024 supreme court ruling would certainly embolden any president looking to push limits and line pockets.
label: SUBJ

text: In Trump v United States, justices decided former presidents have absolute immunity from criminal prosecution for “official acts”.
label: OBJ

text: “This is defined very broadly in the judgment,” says George.
label: OBJ

text: “It also gives the president the power to pardon anyone in the administration,” he says.
label: OBJ

text: “Now, anyone working in the Trump administration who sees an opportunity to make money in a legal grey area or worse, knows Trump can pardon them at the end of his term.”
label: SUBJ

text: George thinks the effects of the ruling go further.
label: OBJ

text: “You know that if you want that pardon in late 2028, you better stay in Trump’s good graces,” he says.
label: OBJ

text: And if Trump now looks invincible to many of his supporters, you can see why: “[He left] the White House in the middle of a pandemic with terrible approval ratings.
label: OBJ

text: There was a riot in the capital.
label: OBJ

text: He [was then] hit with all these charges,” says George.
label: OBJ

text: Going into the 2024 election, Trump stood accused of 91 felony counts.
label: OBJ

text: But then: “He beat the charges and came back to the White House.”
label: OBJ

text: It’s widely understood that, in Washington DC, money talks.
label: SUBJ

text: The US federal lobbying industry was worth billions long before Trump’s presidency.
label: OBJ

text: Wealthy donors are also handed plum jobs by Democrats.
label: SUBJ

text: “This has always been somewhat the case,” says George.
label: OBJ

text: “People in each party raise billions from wealthy individuals and special interests to campaign.
label: OBJ

text: Certain positions are quite clearly patronage jobs.”
label: SUBJ

text: But George distinguishes this from what we are witnessing now: “It’s apples and oranges.
label: OBJ

text: This is the blending of his business pursuits with the business of being president, and allowing people he appoints to enrich themselves as they go about conducting the business of the White House.”
label: SUBJ

text: With the midterms coming in November, Trump’s opponents are looking for leverage.
label: OBJ

text: “Democrats will likely focus on corruption,” he says, “and the potential 2028 candidates already doing so.
label: OBJ

text: If they win with that message and mandate, and control Congress – two big ifs – you can see an early 2029 far-reaching anti-corruption package coming out of Congress.”
label: SUBJ

text: Still, the supreme court’s presidential immunity ruling is going nowhere soon.
label: SUBJ

text: “The bigger question,” says George, “is the extent of the corrosive effect on American democracy, and to what extent people become numb to it.”
label: OBJ

text: In recent months, he has been thinking about the optics Trump creates in the Oval Office.
label: OBJ

text: “When he has cameras in, Trump sits at the desk with all his people standing around him.
label: OBJ

text: It is almost like a king: I am the boss, I will do what I want and take my spoils.”
label: SUBJ

text: A bit of good news to remind you that the world’s not all bad
label: SUBJ

text: It’s a tonic for burnout, anxiety and insomnia, and it’s not the latest peptide or wellness trend.
label: SUBJ

text: Although it can’t cure all ailments, being outdoors in nature is “a great healer”, according to [OUTLET] readers, who responded to a recent study finding that almost half of adults in the UK spend fewer than three hours a week outdoors in nature, and shared stories about why being in nature means so much to them.
label: OBJ

text: Hannah Powell, who has dealt with burnout and functional neurological disorder told us: “I have to look at plants every day.”
label: OBJ

text: David Lynch, meanwhile, says of being outside: “I am more fully human, my whole self.
label: OBJ

text: Anxiety levels drop, all worldly concerns are put in perspective and I feel younger.”
label: OBJ

text: Others shared the gratitude they feel to be in green spaces.
label: OBJ

text: In the words of reader Yve: “I believe that nature and being outdoors is a great healer.”
label: OBJ

text: And finally, [OUTLET]’s puzzles are here to keep you entertained throughout the day. Until tomorrow.

text: President Donald Trump announced that he, “the Number One Attraction anywhere in the World,” will replace multiple performers who bailed on the Freedom 250 concert series.
label: OBJ

text: “I understand Artists are getting ‘the yips’ having to do with their performance on Wednesday, so I am thinking about bringing the Number One Attraction anywhere in the World, the man who gets much larger audiences than Elvis in his prime, and he does so without a guitar, the man who loves our Country more than anyone else, and the man who some say is the Greatest President in History (THE GOAT!), DONALD J. TRUMP, to take the place of these highly paid, Third Rate ‘Artists,’ and give a major speech, rallying the Country forward like I have done ever since being President!”
label: SUBJ

text: he wrote Saturday on Truth Social.
label: OBJ

text: Trump’s announcement comes after a majority of musical acts dropped out of the Great American State Fair, an event celebrating America’s 250th anniversary.
label: OBJ

label: OBJ
