text: Family visitation at the Delaney Hall immigration detention center is being restored to at least part of the facility, New Jersey’s governor and US homeland security officials confirmed on Sunday morning, after a week during which heated demonstrations at the site were met with aggressive policing tactics.
label: OBJ

text: Meanwhile, families of detained immigrants grappled with conflicting information about exactly whom among them would get visitation after the announcement from governor Mikie Sherrill and the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
label: OBJ

text: And local officials by Sunday had also indefinitely imposed an overnight curfew beginning at 9pm for a blocked-off area including Delaney Hall.
label: OBJ

text: Delaney Hall visitation had been canceled after detained immigrants began carrying out an ongoing hunger and labor strike inside the detention center – which prompted protests outside the facility in support of those striking.
label: OBJ

text: Facility staff confirmed to [OUTLET] on Sunday that what are known as units 1 and 3 were given visitation beginning at about noon and 2pm local time, respectively.
label: OBJ

text: Unit 1 is a women’s section of the facility.
label: OBJ

text: Unit 2 is where the majority of the hunger-striking detainees are based, and it was unclear on Sunday whether it would have access to family visitation.
label: OBJ

text: Sherrill’s office and the private prison company GEO Group, which runs the facility, did not respond to a request for comment.
label: OBJ

text: The road leading to Delaney Hall is now fully blocked by police, except for families attempting to visit detained loved ones, state officials announced on Sunday afternoon.
label: OBJ

text: The governor’s announcement and subsequent confusion by families followed a night of violent clashes outside the facility between local officials and protesters.
label: OBJ

text: In the aftermath of that, Newark’s mayor, Ras Baraka, responded by activating a curfew for the area surrounding Delaney Hall.
label: OBJ

text: The curfew would be in place nightly from 9pm to 6am “until further notice”, said Baraka’s office, which threatened arrest or legal action if people did not disperse during that time.
label: OBJ

text: On Sunday morning, Sherrill and other top New Jersey state officials said that three people were arrested on Saturday night as a result of clashes with police.
label: OBJ

text: State officials said those arrest happened after a group of protesters attacked police and a barrier.
label: OBJ

text: The Delaney Hall protests and clashes have become the latest flashpoint in the growing opposition to the aggressive anti-immigrant tactics Donald Trump’s administration has implemented nationwide throughout his second presidency.
label: SUBJ

text: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detains immigrants in its network of facilities across the US while the cases of those detained play out in courts.
label: OBJ

text: ICE detention centers have been repeatedly criticized for harsh conditions.
label: OBJ

text: Hakeem Jeffries, the top US House Democrat of nearby New York, conducted an oversight visit of Delaney Hall on Sunday, and said the conditions of confinement “shock the conscience”.
label: OBJ

text: On 22 May, a group of immigrants detained inside Delaney Hall detention announced a hunger and labor strike inside the facility, demanding improved conditions, medical care, a meeting with Sherrill and for their immigration cases to proceed.
label: OBJ

text: Between 300 and 400 detainees have since participated in the strike.
label: OBJ

text: Protests began shortly thereafter, with lawmakers attempting to visit the facility.
label: OBJ

text: The facility gained further national attention after ICE officers pepper-sprayed US senator Andy Kim, a New Jersey Democrat, outside the facility during a skirmish there on Monday.
label: OBJ

text: ICE officers have used pepper spray as well as stun guns throughout the demonstrations.
label: OBJ

text: They have also shoved and arrested protesters.
label: OBJ

text: On Friday, Sherrill and other top New Jersey officials announced that state police would replace ICE officers outside Delaney Hall.
label: OBJ

text: The state police set up road blocks around half a mile on either side of the detention center.
label: OBJ

text: That night clashes erupted after state police officers began moving in on protesters.
label: OBJ

text: State police officials on horseback moved through the crowd.
label: OBJ

text: Other state police officers in riot gear shot teargas canisters at protesters, aggressively shoving demonstrators and arresting six.
label: SUBJ

text: Advocates present at Delaney Hall on Saturday repeatedly criticized Sherrill, a Democrat, for her response to the protests.
label: OBJ

text: “The escalation that happened [on Friday] was ten times worse than what ICE was doing to everyone prior nights,” Murad Awawdeh, the president and CEO of the New York Immigrant Coalition, said in an interview on Saturday outside of the facility.
label: OBJ

text: “If anything, the escalators were the state police.”
label: OBJ

text: A statement from Sherill on Saturday announcing the restoration of family visitation at Delaney Hall claimed DHS had “met our demand”.
label: OBJ

text: But DHS refuted the governor’s statement.
label: OBJ

text: “To be clear: Visitation was only suspended because of violent riots,” a DHS spokesperson said.
label: OBJ

text: “Now that we have a secure perimeter, visitation can resume.”
label: OBJ

text: Lucas, who was married to “Star Wars” creator George Lucas from 1969 to 1983, died Wednesday from metastatic cancer, attorney Deidre Von Rock said in an email to The Associated Press.
label: OBJ

text: She died in Rancho Mirage, California, surrounded by loved ones, Von Rock said.
label: OBJ

text: Marcia Lucas was the editor on 1983′s “Return of the Jedi” and the pre-“Star Wars” George Lucas-directed films “THX 1138” and “American Graffiti.”
label: OBJ

text: She was also part of the editing team for director Martin Scorsese’s 1970s films “Taxi Driver,” “Alice Doesn’t Live Here Anymore” and “New York, New York.”
label: OBJ

text: Editor was a rare senior creative position where a woman could find a foothold in Hollywood.
label: SUBJ

text: Marcia Lucas became one of several women whose work in the editing chair made sense of the work of the overwhelmingly male directors of the New Hollywood of the late 1960s through the early 1980s, including Dede Allen, editor of “Bonnie and Clyde” and “Dog Day Afternoon”; Verna Fields, editor of “Paper Moon” and “Jaws”; and Thelma Schoonmaker, editor of most of Scorsese’s films starting with 1980′s “Raging Bull.”
label: OBJ

text: Lucas was often called the unsung hero of “Star Wars,” the original film that after sequels, prequels and spinoffs has come to be known by its subtitle, “A New Hope.”
label: OBJ

text: She convinced her then-husband that he should have Obi-Wan Kenobi, played by Alec Guinness, die in his lightsaber battle with Darth Vader and become a spirit guide to Mark Hamill’s Luke Skywalker.
label: OBJ

text: And she had to make sense of raw footage that could have been a mess in the wrong hands, including the climactic rebel attack on the Death Star.
label: OBJ

text: “It was extremely complex and we had 40,000 feet of dialogue footage of pilots saying this and that.
label: OBJ

text: And she had to cull through all that, and put in all the fighting as well,” George Lucas told Rolling Stone in an interview a few months after the film came out.
label: OBJ

text: “Nobody really has ever tried to interweave an actual plot story into a dogfight, and we were trying to do that.”
label: OBJ

text: Lucas was born Marcia Griffin in Modesto, California shortly after the end of World War II.
label: OBJ

text: She moved to Los Angeles with her mother after her parents divorced when she was a small child.
label: OBJ

text: She began working as a film librarian and moved into working as an editor on commercials, trailers and promotional films.
label: OBJ

text: She was an assistant editor on the documentary “Journey to the Pacific” for Fields, who also hired George Lucas, then a film student at the University of Southern California.
label: OBJ

text: The couple became engaged soon after.
label: OBJ

text: Their marriage would essentially end in 1982, but they kept their divorce under wraps until after the release of “Return of the Jedi” in 1983.
label: OBJ

text: Marcia Lucas was then married to Tom Rodrigues, a production manager at the Skywalker Ranch production center, from 1983 to 1993.
label: OBJ

text: She is survived by her daughters, Amanda Lucas and Amy Soper, and grandchildren Felix Hallikainen, Aeliana Hallikainen and Knox Soper.
label: OBJ

text: “Her influence on film is indelible, but those who knew her best will remember the way she made life feel more vivid, more beautiful, more fun, and more full of love,” a family statement said.
label: OBJ

text: “Her work was known for its emotional intelligence, rhythm, and humanity — a rare ability to find the truth of a scene and bring heart, momentum, and clarity to the screen.”
label: OBJ

text: What does it cost to age with dignity?
label: SUBJ

text: It’s an urgent question as the youngest baby boomers approach 65 and their adult children prepare to take on their care.
label: SUBJ

text: I’m one of those adult children – visiting my dad, with advanced dementia, every week in the memory care facility where he has lived for a year, costing $8,500 a month out of pocket.
label: OBJ

text: I also live with my mom, who just got a knee replacement and deals with a chronic autoimmune illness.
label: OBJ

text: Like many women of her generation, she had little paid work after my brother and I were born in the early 80s.
label: OBJ

text: Women aged 65 and older are roughly 80% more likely to live in poverty than men are, in large part because of the caretaking they provided.
label: OBJ

text: My dad, raised in a family that constantly went broke and declared bankruptcy, became a bankruptcy lawyer himself and broke his promises with my mom of equal parenting.
label: OBJ

text: Workaholism was his emotional safety blanket after an erratic childhood in which he had to answer the door to debt collectors and lie that his parents weren’t home as they hid in a back room of their Denver bungalow.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s unlikely my parents’ savings will run out, but savings born of trauma (plus some white male privilege) shouldn’t be a prerequisite for having the care you need in your twilight years.
label: SUBJ

text: Unsurprisingly, cultural messages about ageing and savings in the US are riddled with rugged individualism.
label: SUBJ

text: Our parents should buy long-term care insurance; in reality, only 3-4% of those over 50 have it (my parents don’t).
label: SUBJ

text: Our parents should save for retirement; the truth is that 46% of Americans have no retirement savings at all, and those who do have an average of $955, a far cry from estimates that say most of us will need $1.5m to retire comfortably.
label: SUBJ

text: This is not a color-blind consideration: white families accumulate more wealth over time than Black or Hispanic families; in their 70s, the average white family had more than four times the wealth of the average Black family.
label: SUBJ

text: Even if you did attempt to build wealth, our current elder care system, if one can even call it that, pushes you into a Sophie’s choice in your so-called golden years.
label: SUBJ

text: The “forgotten middle”, as researchers have begun calling them, have an annual income and accumulated savings that are too high to qualify for Medicaid, but too low to afford the care they need as they age.
label: OBJ

text: These elders are often forced to spend down their savings so they can be destitute enough to qualify for Medicaid.
label: SUBJ

text: If our parents didn’t buy long-term care insurance or save for retirement, they are cast as irresponsible.
label: SUBJ

text: They’ve made their bed in a dingy, overcrowded skilled nursing facility, as it were, and now they have to lie in it.
label: SUBJ

text: If they saved just enough to buy a little home – perhaps their one special symbol of the American dream – they have to sell it now in order to get care, leaving their children nothing in the end (or maybe a big pile of debt).
label: SUBJ

text: In other words, the gap between what should happen in some theoretical economic climate and what is happening in the real one is, preposterously, being blamed on our parents regardless of their demographic or educational background.
label: SUBJ

text: The biggest American generation is now largely past retirement age, and shame, in addition to being immoral, is inadequate.
label: SUBJ

text: Boomers haven’t saved enough, and most didn’t even have the chance in the first place.
label: SUBJ

text: Now how about we cut the bootstrap rhetoric and get real about how to invest in collective solutions that give our elders the last years they deserve, create good, humane jobs for professional caregivers, and let family caregivers avoid such dramatic stress and debt?
label: SUBJ

text: I’ve got a few ideas, having survived a gauntlet of elder care crises in the last few years myself: Day programs.
label: OBJ

text: Day programs. Day programs for elders, like those for kids, are a fantastic community resource.
label: SUBJ

text: Publicly funded transportation can take elders to a center designed with their joy and capacity in mind.
label: SUBJ

text: My dad went to one such program and it was a balm; he sang karaoke, he saw the on-staff nurse when needed, and I was able to get some work done without him joining my Zoom calls.
label: OBJ

text: What’s more, according to recent estimates, the median day program costs $100 a day v about $200 for assisted living and more than $200 for in-home care.
label: OBJ

text: Day programs, vastly underfunded in most states, are a great way to keep elders ageing in place, prevent loneliness, and make sure family caregivers don’t burn out or have to quit their jobs. Worker-owned home healthcare.
label: SUBJ

text: Worker-owned home healthcare. There’s a care workforce shortage for good reason; too many of these jobs aren’t good jobs.
label: SUBJ

text: One small but growing part of the home healthcare market consists of worker-owned cooperatives, where professional caregivers are the leaders of their own organizations – setting hourly rates, vacation and sick leave policies, and training approaches.
label: OBJ

text: These organizations are shown to have far better worker retention than traditional care companies and, of course, it’s a boon to family caregivers to know that the person taking care of their loved one feels empowered and will stick around.
label: SUBJ

text: Public long-term care insurance.
label: OBJ

text: Washington is the first state in the country to create public long-term care for its full-time workers, WACares.
label: OBJ

text: By contributing a small amount (0.58%) from wages to the fund, Washingtonians earn a long-term care benefit (up to $36,500) for when they need it.
label: OBJ

text: This could be a test case for other states that want to be humane places for people to age.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s time to acknowledge that collective solutions – not shame and blame, piled on top of longstanding inequities – are the key to get us through a tender time in our family’s lives.
label: SUBJ

text: How we handle elder care in the next 20 years is going to shape the future of this country in innumerable ways; our economic and ethical future hangs in the balance.
label: SUBJ

text: Courtney E Martin has a weekly newsletter called Examined Family.
label: OBJ

text: Her most recent book is Learning in Public:
label: OBJ

text: Lessons for a Racially Divided America From My Daughter’s School
label: SUBJ

text: Trump has warned that Cuba “is next” after U.S. military forces captured Venezuela’s autocratic leader, Nicolás Maduro, in a January raid.
label: OBJ

text: In the months since, the Trump administration has imposed an oil blockade on Cuba, maintained warships in the Caribbean Sea and indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro on federal charges.
label: OBJ

text: Gen. Francis Donovan, head of U.S. Southern Command, met with Lt. Gen. Roberto Legrá Sotolongo and other Cuban military officials.
label: OBJ

text: Cuba’s Revolutionary Armed Forces said in a statement that both sides viewed “the meeting positively because it addressed security issues along the perimeter separating the military enclave, and they agreed to maintain communication between the two military commands.”
label: OBJ

text: Top Trump aides, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and CIA chief John Ratcliffe, also have met with Cuban officials to explore possible improvements in relations.
label: OBJ

text: But the U.S. side has come away unimpressed from those talks, leading to even more sanctions imposed on the Cuban government.
label: SUBJ

text: Besides the meeting, Donovan also assessed the security of the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay and discussed the “safety of service members and their families, and operational readiness with base officials,” U.S. Southern Command said in a post on X.
label: OBJ

text: The U.S. maintains the base despite decades of friction with Cuba’s socialist leaders, whom Trump wants removed from power.
label: OBJ

text: The U.S. military has a handful of Navy ships, including at least one amphibious assault ship, in the Caribbean, a much smaller force than was present at the time of the Maduro raid.
label: OBJ
