text: Texas could become the hottest battleground state in the country, if the results of both Republican and Democratic primaries are anything to go by.
label: SUBJ

text: Democrat James Talarico, a progressive Presbyterian seminarian, will face off against Trump’s favored candidate, the scandal-plagued attorney general, Ken Paxton.
label: SUBJ

text: The matchup has liberals salivating.
label: SUBJ

text: Paxton, dogged by corruption charges, impeachment hearings and an affair that left his marriage in tatters, is considered by some in his own party as “the worst possible top-of-the-ticket” candidate.
label: SUBJ

text: Meanwhile, Talarico, a fresh-faced, clean-cut millennial, who quotes scripture to justify his progressive beliefs, seems like the perfect foil, at least according to Democratic party leaders.
label: SUBJ

text: No wonder, then, that Talarico pulled in a massive fundraising haul immediately after Paxton won his party’s nomination.
label: SUBJ

text: This combined with his already impressive war-chest of about $27m is a good indication that Democratic donors are betting big on Talarico to turn Texas blue.
label: SUBJ

text: But the reality is that blue-collar voters, not blue-blooded donors, will decide the outcome of the race.
label: SUBJ

text: And Talarico has a lot of work to do to win over working-class Texans.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s true that a bevy of early polls show Talarico slightly ahead.
label: SUBJ

text: But if you dig into the results you’ll notice that these surveys skew toward highly engaged and highly educated voters.
label: SUBJ

text: Consider a recent poll from Public Policy Polling that has Talarico leading Paxton by seven points; only 22% of voters sampled have less than a college education.
label: OBJ

text: Or a recent University of Texas poll which has Talarico up eight points; only 27% of respondents lack a degree.
label: OBJ

text: Polls like these could be giving Democrats a false sense of confidence by overrepresenting college-educated voters who increasingly skew liberal.
label: SUBJ

text: Indeed, in the primary election Talarico did best with highly educated voters, and abysmally with those voters who lack any college experience.
label: SUBJ

text: And while primary contests are different animals than a general election, those trends could be cause for alarm.
label: SUBJ

text: The fact is Texas is a blue-collar state.
label: SUBJ

text: More than 60% of voters there do not have a college degree.
label: OBJ

text: Winning the working-class vote isn’t optional.
label: SUBJ

text: To be sure, Talarico should be applauded for focusing his campaign on the issues that matter most to ordinary Texans: the rising cost of living and the top-heavy gilded age inequality that keeps us stuck in a low-growth, low-wage economy.
label: SUBJ

text: Working-class voters appreciate these kinds of progressive economic appeals, and many of the policies that Talarico advocates are broadly popular.
label: SUBJ

text: But this alone may not be enough to sway a jaded and increasingly frustrated working class.
label: SUBJ

text: Talarico’s personal style is far more professorial than populist. That’s not surprising.
label: SUBJ

text: That’s not surprising. He has a master’s degree from Harvard University and worked as a non-profit “ed-tech” executive before his career in politics.
label: OBJ

text: At times, he sounds as if he’s doing an impersonation of Barack Obama: “I ran for office not to be served [pause] but to serve …” In television interviews he looks wooden and rehearsed, spouting cliches and spinning tough questions.
label: SUBJ

text: Despite campaigning against the billionaires and corrupt elites, he comes off as the consummate Washington insider.
label: SUBJ

text: What about his unique brand of Christianity?
label: SUBJ

text: Democrats don’t ordinarily run seminarians for the Senate.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s almost as if Texas liberals, outnumbered and aware of the yawning red-blue divide – especially on the questions of faith and flag – elected a preacher imagining he could have magic appeal to Trump-exhausted Republicans and swingy Texas Latinos.
label: SUBJ

text: That’s to say, it’s hard to shake the feeling that it’s something of a political act.
label: SUBJ

text: He ought to be careful here.
label: SUBJ

text: The only thing more condescending than assuming that religious people are provincial, and therefore stupid, is assuming that because they are religious they would surely follow a preacher.
label: SUBJ

text: Worse, his fluency in foundation-funded progressivism has led him to embrace a kind of cultural politics that appeal only to genteel Austin-area liberals and virtually no one else.
label: SUBJ

text: He’s been hammered by Republican attack ads for saying “God is nonbinary.”
label: OBJ

text: Talarico insists that the quote was taken out of context, though I can’t imagine the context in which it would be an intelligent thing to say.
label: SUBJ

text: Liberals might find such attacks unfair but they could do real damage.
label: SUBJ

text: Ultimately, these faults are not his own.
label: OBJ

text: They are downstream from a larger social problem and symptoms of a broader Democratic party illness.
label: SUBJ

text: The trouble is that the party’s leadership, and its major decision-makers, are overwhelmingly drawn from the world of white-collar foundations and white-shoe law firms.
label: SUBJ

text: As a result, liberal leaders have separated themselves from the popular classes.
label: SUBJ

text: So much so that even when they have begun to swallow (with great indigestion) a populist message, they can’t help but regurgitate the slogans in a dialect that smacks snobbish.
label: SUBJ

text: Unfortunately for him, that will be a liability among blue-collar voters who much prefer working-class candidates to manicured Ivy-educated professionals.
label: SUBJ

text: Of course, he can’t change who he is.
label: SUBJ

text: He can, instead, speak intelligently about the issues.
label: SUBJ

text: In this regard, he benefits from his opponent’s faults.
label: OBJ

text: Paxton, a millionaire backed by billionaires, is the epitome of a corrupt and out-of-touch elite.
label: SUBJ

text: He has, it seems, no meaningful solutions to the crises that face blue-collar Texans or the American working class more broadly.
label: SUBJ

text: His campaign materials routinely attack former president Joe Biden, seemingly unaware of who is in the White House.
label: SUBJ

text: As for his economic program, it consists of making America “the crypto capital of the world” by loosening regulations and “empowering crypto innovators”.
label: OBJ

text: Whatever that might mean, it won’t help lower the cost of a new home or bring back family-sustaining wages.
label: SUBJ

text: Still, Paxton poses as a populist who has been battered by the Washington DC establishment; while many working-class voters are fed up with Trump, Texas is still a conservative state, and his kind of populism could have real purchase.
label: SUBJ

text: For Talarico to take full-advantage of Paxton’s failings, he ought to be more programmatic.
label: SUBJ

text: Yes, “affordability” is all the rage in liberal circles.
label: SUBJ

text: But what’s missing from that agenda, if one could even call a loose collection of poll-tested talking points an agenda, is a coherent theory of how to bring back high-wage American jobs.
label: SUBJ

text: Cutting Trump’s tariffs and expanding a few tax breaks is far too timid a program to address the causes of our economic sclerosis – causes Talarico himself admirably identifies: lopsided wealth inequality, stagnant wage growth, depressed demand.
label: SUBJ

text: Instead of a one-sided (and ultimately futile) focus on cutting consumer costs, Talarico ought to put forward a program that focuses more on what it will take to significantly raise wages for millions of workers.
label: SUBJ

text: Instead of a kneejerk anti-tariff position he ought to outline what a world after globalism might look like.
label: SUBJ

text: What we need is not one policy or slogan but a new productivist vision of our future economy.
label: SUBJ

text: Texas happens to be just the place to make such a case.
label: SUBJ

text: In the last year, the state experienced the largest building boom in the country.
label: OBJ

text: Building-trades unions have grown in leaps and bounds as a result.
label: SUBJ

text: The growth is largely the product of huge datacenter buildouts.
label: OBJ

text: Those buildouts have been controversial, but the development demonstrates the power of big infrastructure investment to raise the wages of thousands of workers practically overnight.
label: SUBJ

text: There is no reason such investments ought to be limited to those made by our Silicon Valley and Wall Street overlords.
label: SUBJ

text: Generational public investments in new energy generation; to unite and upgrade the electrical grid; to fix crumbling roads, schools buildings, bridges and tunnels could have the very same effect.
label: SUBJ

text: Policies like these combined with a jobs-first trade agenda and a Made-in-America industrial policy could give the labor movement the leverage it needs to strengthen worker wages across the state.
label: SUBJ

text: And they would go a long way to persuade working-class Texans that Talarico is the man for the job.
label: SUBJ

text: Rep. Suhas Subramanyam (D-Va.) shed more light on former Attorney General Pam Bondi’s much-anticipated hearing with the House Oversight Committee on Friday, claiming she was “combative throughout” while also defending not meeting with the survivors of Jeffrey Epstein.
label: OBJ

text: “She said that it made no sense to meet with the survivors for some reason.
label: OBJ

text: ‘It wouldn’t be appropriate,’ was the word she used,” Subramanyam told CNN’s Boris Sanchez.
label: OBJ

text: Subramanyam added that the former AG said she “didn’t seem to have any awareness of the survivors reaching out to her wanting to meet, or reaching out to her about wanting their own files, their own statements that they made to the DOJ.”
label: OBJ

text: Bondi, who has been a private citizen since being ousted from her position at the DOJ in April, appeared on Capitol Hill Friday following a bipartisan subpoena issued by the Oversight Committee.
label: OBJ

text: Her interview came under fire, as critics argued that it should have been a sworn, videotaped deposition instead.
label: OBJ

text: “We didn’t even want her at a hearing because we saw what she did with the Judiciary Committee,” Subramanyam said.
label: OBJ

text: “But the reality is the oversight Republicans continue to want the witnesses from the administration to be in the best situation possible, not having to answer certain questions, not being on tape, for instance.”
label: OBJ

text: The lawmaker noted that Bondi “also seemed very uncomfortable with her questions.”
label: OBJ

text: Some displeased survivors also appeared in the nation’s capital to voice their frustrations with Bondi, including Dani Bensky, who claimed that “so far the DOJ has done everything they possibly can to intimidate survivors.”
label: OBJ

text: Bondi had struck another nerve with the survivors during a hearing in February following the DOJ’s botched release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, which redacted a majority of the convicted sex offender’s alleged co-conspirators, but named some of the survivors.
label: SUBJ

text: At the time, Bondi refused to apologize or even acknowledge any of the survivors standing behind her when urged to by Democratic lawmakers.
label: OBJ

text: “So they were in the room with her standing, and she said that she didn’t think it was appropriate for them to meet, that she wasn’t aware of their efforts?” Sanchez asked.
label: OBJ

text: Sanchez asked. “At one point, she said ...
label: OBJ

text: she didn’t think it was appropriate to meet with survivors,” Subramanyam said.
label: OBJ

text: “At another point, she seemed not to recall their outreach to her office, and she didn’t seem to recall a lot of things, even things that happened very recently.”
label: OBJ

text: I do not wish Trump ill.
label: OBJ

text: While he hasn’t shown a shred of compassion for anyone other than himself, this doesn’t justify any of us lacking compassion for him.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s also in the interest of the US and the world that he be physically and mentally able to discharge the duties of his office.
label: SUBJ

text: So we have reason to be concerned about Trump’s visit to Walter Reed national military medical center last week for what the White House described as a “routine annual dental and medical assessment”.
label: SUBJ

text: (A memo from Trump’s physician released over the weekend by the White House said Trump “remains in excellent health.”)
label: OBJ

text: Trump turns 80 next month.
label: OBJ

text: I feel entitled to comment on the practical meaning of this milestone because I’ll also turn 80 next month (he was born 10 days before me).
label: OBJ

text: Let’s just say that reaching it doesn’t mean altogether good things, unless you consider the alternative.
label: SUBJ

text: Even in a healthy person, small things begin to break down as one approaches 80.
label: SUBJ

text: Everything takes just a bit more time and effort. Joints ache.
label: OBJ

text: Joints ache. Energy isn’t quite as abundant.
label: OBJ

text: The 80-year-old mind isn’t as quick.
label: SUBJ

text: The frontal lobe’s capacity to remember names goes to shit.
label: SUBJ

text: (Yesterday, I could barely remember the name of a garage mechanic whom I’ve known for nearly half a century.)
label: OBJ

text: Taken separately, such minor frailties typically mount to no more than a personal frustration.
label: SUBJ

text: In a president of the United States, they can pose a major challenge to the nation and world.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump frequently proclaims he’s in excellent health.
label: OBJ

text: “Just finished my 6 month physical at Walter Reed Military Medical Center.
label: OBJ

text: Everything checked out PERFECTLY,” he wrote on Truth Social early Tuesday afternoon.
label: OBJ

text: “Thank you to the great Doctors and Staff!
label: SUBJ

text: Heading back to the White House.”
label: OBJ

text: But even “PERFECTLY” is a relative concept for someone ending his seventh decade and beginning his eighth, who’s the oldest person to assume the presidency and the second-oldest to hold the office.
label: SUBJ

text: (Joe Biden was 82 when he left in 2025.)
label: OBJ

text: Presidents aren’t legally required to release their medical records but, given the effluvium of lies in which Trump permanently floats, we’d be excused if we didn’t entirely trust this “PERFECTLY” report.
label: SUBJ

text: Plus, there are his bruised hands, swollen ankles, bouts of drowsiness, exceedingly long blinks during official meetings (some call them “naps”) and erratic – if not off-the-charts weird – behavior.
label: SUBJ

text: Add in the frequency of his health “checkups”.
label: SUBJ

text: Tuesday’s visit to Walter Reed was Trump’s third in-person doctor’s visit in a little more than a year.
label: OBJ

text: His first physical of this term of office was in mid-April last year.
label: OBJ

text: He returned in early October for a “semiannual physical”.
label: OBJ

text: In early January, he had what was described as a brief dental appointment.
label: OBJ

text: Earlier this month, another dental appointment.
label: OBJ

text: Followed by his return to Walter Reed on Tuesday for his third physical in 13 months.
label: OBJ

text: Consider also the shifting explanations.
label: SUBJ

text: In July, navy Capt Sean Barbabella, Trump’s physician, explained that the bruises on Trump’s right hand were “consistent with minor soft tissue irritation from frequent handshaking”.
label: OBJ

text: The explanation seemed plausible until the bruises spread to his left hand.
label: SUBJ

text: There’s also the changing story about Trump’s scans.
label: SUBJ

text: In December, he told reporters that he’d had an MRI in October, but wasn’t sure what part of his body was scanned.
label: OBJ

text: “It wasn’t the brain,” he said, defensively, “because I took a cognitive test and I aced it.”
label: OBJ

text: Barbabella then issued a memo explaining it had been a scan of his heart and abdomen, and that in both cases the imaging was “perfectly normal”.
label: OBJ

text: In January, Trump altered his story to say it was a CT scan rather than an MRI. Why?
label: SUBJ

text: Why? Trump being Trump, presumably he doesn’t want anyone to know anything about his health that might reveal something he fears enemies and critics might see as a weakness.
label: SUBJ

text: “In retrospect, it’s too bad I took [the scan] because it gave them a little ammunition,” Trump said.
label: OBJ

text: “I would have been a lot better off if [I] didn’t, because the fact that I took it said: ‘Oh gee, is something wrong?’ Well, nothing’s wrong.”
label: OBJ

text: Well, nothing’s wrong.” What’s he afraid of?
label: SUBJ

text: Probably that the American public will catch on to his diminishing capacities.
label: SUBJ

text: Three years ago, according to a Washington Post/ABC News/Ipsos poll, only 28% of the public thought Trump insufficiently healthy to hold the nation’s highest office.
label: OBJ

text: This month, the same poll found that 55% of the public thought his health insufficient for him to serve effectively.
label: OBJ

text: Behind the public’s mounting worries is a growing sense that Trump isn’t mentally all there.
label: SUBJ

text: Physical and mental health aren’t easily separated, especially as one reaches 80.
label: SUBJ

text: I often can’t remember where I put my wallet and keys or why I’ve entered a room.
label: OBJ

text: I also have less patience than I used to have.
label: OBJ

text: (I’m less tolerant of long lines, automated phone menus and Republicans.)
label: OBJ

text: But if Trump can’t remember where he put, say, a top-secret memo or why he entered the Situation Room, or if he expresses bizarre impatience, it’s a potential risk to the nation and world.
label: SUBJ

text: “Open the Fuckin’ Strait, you crazy bastards, or you’ll be living in Hell,” Trump exploded Easter morning, adding an Islamic prayer to the end of the post.
label: OBJ

text: The following Tuesday, he threatened that unless Iran struck a deal in 12 hours, its whole civilization would die.
label: OBJ

text: When Iran shot down two US airmen, aides who were getting minute-by-minute updates reportedly kept Trump out of the Situation Room because they believed his impatience wouldn’t be helpful, a senior administration official said.
label: OBJ

text: Then came Trump’s rant against the pope.
label: SUBJ

text: Pope Leo is WEAK on Crime, and terrible for Foreign Policy.
label: SUBJ

text: … I like his brother Louis much better than I like him, because Louis is all MAGA.
label: SUBJ

text: He gets it, and Leo doesn’t! …
label: SUBJ

text: Leo should get his act together as Pope, use Common Sense, stop catering to the Radical Left, and focus on being a Great Pope, not a Politician.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s hurting him very badly and, more importantly, it’s hurting the Catholic Church!
label: SUBJ

text: During a subsequent Q&A with reporters, Trump doubled down: “I don’t think he’s doing a very good job.
label: OBJ

text: He likes crime, I guess.
label: SUBJ

text: … I am not a fan of Pope Leo.”
label: SUBJ

text: Days later, Trump posted an AI-generated portrait of himself as a kind of American Jesus.
label: OBJ

text: When this caused a wave of criticism and outrage (much of it from fundamentalist Christians), he insisted he was portraying himself “as a doctor, making people better”.
label: OBJ

text: Rather than helping Republicans in the upcoming midterm elections by, for example, embarking on an “affordability tour” (as White House aides have urged him to do), Trump has been on a “revenge tour” against Republican members of Congress he deems insufficiently loyal – a gambit that may cost Republicans dearly in the midterms.
label: SUBJ

text: Yet Trump insisted at a cabinet meeting that “I don’t care about the midterms”.
label: OBJ

text: He was referring at that moment to Iranian officials who “thought they were going to outwait me” by relying on mounting political pressures to force him to give up, but he might as well have been talking about the blowback from his revenge tour.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump ended the abinet meeting with further evidence of his mental decline, in another rant against Somali Americans.
label: SUBJ

text: “The Somalians, what they’ve done to Minnesota, the Somalians, crooked as hell.
label: SUBJ

text: Ilhan Omar, crooked as hell,” he said, in reference to the Democratic representative from Minnesota.
label: OBJ

text: “They’re all crooks, and we got them, we got them.
label: OBJ

text: Now we’re putting the clamps on.”
label: OBJ

text: His antipathy toward Somali Americans is growing, along with his troubling and erratic behavior.
label: SUBJ

text: In December, weeks before ICE went on a rampage in Minneapolis, Trump had claimed that Somalis made Minnesota a “hellhole”, saying: “The Somalians should be out of here.
label: OBJ

text: They’ve destroyed our country.”
label: SUBJ

text: Of Somalia-born Omar, Trump said: “She shouldn’t be allowed to be a congresswoman, and I’m sure people are looking at that.
label: OBJ

text: She should be thrown the hell out of our country.”
label: SUBJ

text: A day earlier, he called the representative “garbage”, saying he didn’t want Somalis in the US.
label: OBJ

text: Can you imagine any other president of the United States singling out a group of foreign-born Americans like this? Of course not.
label: SUBJ

text: Of course not. The evidence continues to mount.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump is clearly incapable of satisfactorily discharging the duties of president of the United States.
label: SUBJ

text: The sooner the 25th amendment is invoked, or he is impeached, the safer are America and the world.
label: SUBJ

text: Cigarette smoking is a risk factor for lung cancer, heart disease and stroke, and it’s long been considered the leading cause of preventable death.
label: OBJ

text: The preliminary findings from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention were based on survey responses from more than 24,200 adults.
label: OBJ

text: In the survey, CDC officials defined current cigarette smoking as smoking at least 100 cigarettes in a lifetime and now smoking every day or some days.
label: OBJ

text: In the mid-1960s, 42% of U.S. adults were smokers.
label: OBJ

text: The rate has been gradually dropping for decades, due to cigarette taxes, tobacco product price hikes, smoking bans, public education campaigns and changes in the social acceptability of lighting up in public.
label: OBJ

text: In 2024, the percentage of current adult smokers fell below 10% for the first time.
label: OBJ

text: Last year, it was 9%, according to the new survey.
label: OBJ

text: The use of electronic cigarettes has been inching up among adults, but has held about steady in 2025, at about 7%.
label: OBJ

text: “The continued decline in smoking is a monumental public health achievement that has saved millions of lives and billions in healthcare costs,” said Yolonda Richardson, president and chief executive of the Campaign for Tobacco-Free Kids, a Washington, D.C.-based advocacy and research organization.
label: OBJ

text: Richardson said current smoking-prevention efforts have been set back by cuts President Donald Trump’s administration made that eliminated the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention’s Office on Smoking and Health and its “Tips from Former Smokers” advertising campaign.
label: OBJ

text: She cited estimates that the “Tips” campaign alone helped more than 1 million Americans quit smoking and saved over $7.3 billion in healthcare costs.
label: OBJ

text: “This critical work must be restored and sustained to continue reducing smoking-related disease, death and healthcare costs nationwide,” Richardson said.
label: OBJ

text: The Associated Press Health and Science Department receives support from the Howard Hughes Medical Institute’s Department of Science Education and the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.
label: OBJ

text: The AP is solely responsible for all content.
label: OBJ

text: The defeat of John Cornyn is a milestone in the downfall of the Republican party.
label: SUBJ

text: His virtue for decades as a “steady conservative institutionalist”, as the New York Times described him, became his terminal liability.
label: OBJ

text: His expenditure of $92m, the greatest amount ever dropped by a candidate in a Senate primary, could not forestall his humiliation at the hands of the scoundrel Ken Paxton, with his lengthy rap sheet of allegations of bribery, abuse of office, felony securities fraud and impeachment by the Republican-controlled Texas House, along with his hostile divorce by his wife on “biblical grounds”.
label: SUBJ

text: Despite Cornyn’s blast of TV ads against “Crooked Ken”, the “Home Wrecker”, Paxton, carrying the imprimatur of Donald Trump, trounced him by 28 points.
label: SUBJ

text: Immediately after the primary, the National Republican Senatorial Committee, which Cornyn had once led, set about scrubbing the ads as if there had been no Cornyn campaign at all and the villainous Paxton was the rightful successor to hold the Senate seat Cornyn had occupied for 24 years.
label: SUBJ

text: The Orwellian erasure was a further measure of the relentless Trump effort to stamp out of existence the remnants of the old party and to build on its ashes his golden idol.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s ignominious rejection is not his alone.
label: SUBJ

text: His loss represents the ongoing shattering of the Republican party whose foundations were laid by Ronald Reagan, laboriously built in Texas by the Bushes, both father and son, with their operative Karl Rove, and, within the Senate, where Cornyn arrived in 2002, the ruling Republican structure established by Mitch McConnell.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn rode on the Reagan wave that swept aside Democrats in Texas, to be raised up as a factotum of the Bush operation, and serve as the indispensable conduit of funds from the oil and gas industry to fuel McConnell’s dark money machine that financed Republican candidates, destroyed campaign finance reform, and secured the conservative majority on the supreme court.
label: SUBJ

text: John Cornyn is an emblematic Republican of the interregnum between Reagan and Trump, shifting its identity from a conservative party for whom the business of America was business to a cult of personality smashing everyone and everything in its path to erect a kleptocracy and a ballroom.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn, a stalwart of the old order, could not remove the fatal flaw of his past.
label: SUBJ

text: John Cornyn first met Karl Rove in the early 1980s.
label: OBJ

text: By then, Rove was the most influential political consultant in Texas, the “boy genius”, as he was called, of the state’s emergent Republican party.
label: SUBJ

text: He had been selected chair of the College Republicans in 1973 by George HW Bush when he was Republican National Committee chair.
label: OBJ

text: By 1977, Rove was made the executive director of the political action committee run by James A Baker that became the basis of Bush’s presidential campaign in 1980.
label: OBJ

text: Two years earlier, Rove had been the political adviser to George W Bush in his failed congressional campaign and to William Clements, the first Republican elected governor of Texas in more than 100 years.
label: OBJ

text: Rove created a political machine based on his direct mail firm, electing dozens of Republicans to statewide office.
label: SUBJ

text: He developed a massive fundraising operation generating tens of millions of dollars by promoting the conservative cause of “tort reform”, working with corporate clients, among them insurance companies, the medical industry, and Phillip Morris and the tobacco lobby, to battle trial lawyers representing consumers pursuing lawsuits.
label: SUBJ

text: Rove enlisted Cornyn as a 32-year-old lawyer through his “tort reform” movement, and got him slated as a state district judge in 1984.
label: OBJ

text: Rove was determined to take over the Texas supreme court, had won three of its nine seats by 1988, and recruited Cornyn to run for the court in 1990.
label: OBJ

text: In 1998, Rove anointed Cornyn to run for state attorney general and helped raise more than $6m from his corporate allies to oust the incumbent Democrat who was going after the tobacco giants.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn was swept in on a Republican wave that re-elected George W Bush governor, setting up his 2000 presidential campaign.
label: OBJ

text: With Rove’s assistance, Cornyn as attorney general co-founded the national Republican Attorneys General Association, which raised millions for Republican attorney general campaigns across the country.
label: OBJ

text: In 2002, Rove worked to make Cornyn a US senator, to fill the seat vacated by Phil Gramm, who had been a Rove client.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn campaigned as a loyal member of “Team Bush”.
label: OBJ

text: In 2006, in Bush’s second midterm, the Republicans lost the Senate.
label: OBJ

text: With Bush fading as an unpopular lame duck, Cornyn was adopted by a new sponsor, Mitch McConnell, now the Senate minority leader.
label: SUBJ

text: In 2009, McConnell put Cornyn in charge of the National Republican Senatorial Committee, where he raised more than $100m to enable the Republicans to retake the Senate in 2010.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn was indeed a very popular man within the Senate Republican Conference and in 2012 McConnell made him the whip.
label: OBJ

text: McConnell’s great causes were the destruction of campaign finance reform and packing the federal courts.
label: SUBJ

text: His fundraising technique was more or less the same that Rove had applied to “tort reform” – a virtuous cycle of money, elections and judges: the corporations would finance the Republicans, who would approve federal judges who, in turn, would be favorable to the corporations.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn preserved and protected the system that grew out of the supreme court’s Citizens United 2010 decision, which allowed unlimited and unaccounted corporate campaign contributions.
label: SUBJ

text: In the Senate, Cornyn battled the Democrats’ Disclose ACT, which would establish transparency about the identity of donors.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn successfully killed it by attaching riders to appropriations bills that restricted the Securities and Exchange Commission from making disclosure rules about campaign donations.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn would raise at least $415m over the years for the Republican Pacs, becoming the top fundraiser after McConnell himself.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn, however, never constructed his own fiefdom, but instead integrated everything he did into McConnell’s operation.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s and McConnell’s networks were one and the same.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn’s operations also were tightly linked into the series of Pacs run by Leonard Leo, chair of the Federalist Society, selector of conservative judges, a tangle closely tied into McConnell’s groups.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s former chief of staff Monica Popp headed a dark money group called the Conservative Majority Project, which in 2024 distributed $1.5m to Right Vote – a conservative non-profit mainly funded by Leo’s Concord Fund.
label: OBJ

text: McConnell tolerated Trump in his first term so long as he churned out Federalist Society approved judges.
label: SUBJ

text: But after Trump’s failed coup of January 6, the frail McConnell, who was in physical danger during the assault on the Capitol, denounced Trump, whom he considered “despicable,” certified the 2020 election of Joe Biden, and scoffed at Trump as politically finished, which was the reason he made sure Republicans did not remove Trump from office in the post-January 6 impeachment process.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump unleashed a string of insults at McConnell as a “broken down hack” and “Old Crow”, and targeted McConnell’s wife, Elaine Chao, who was Trump’s Secretary of Transportation, as McConnell’s “China loving wife, Coco Chow!”
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn, in McConnell’s shadow, and, before that, in Bush’s, could not help but become collateral damage.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump’s rage eventually engulfed him – “VERY disloyal to me, as President,” Trump tweeted.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn’s original sin in Trump’s mind was to certify the 2020 election, though he voted to acquit Trump in the impeachment case.
label: SUBJ

text: He had followed the lead of McConnell.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn desperately tried to compensate in his re-election bid by posting a picture of himself staring at an open copy in his lap of The Art of the Deal as though he was absorbing its wisdom, then posing in front of a Trump-themed joint in Houston run by a Maga fanatic called Trump Burger, and, Cornyn’s coup de grace of flattery, introducing a bill that Interstate Highway 47 be renamed “Trump Interstate”.
label: SUBJ

text: The toadyism apparently had the reverse effect of inciting Trump’s animosity.
label: SUBJ

text: He endorsed the miscreant Paxton as “a true MAGA Warrior who has ALWAYS delivered for Texas”.
label: OBJ

text: Cornyn could not transfer his traditional method of obsequious advancement to Trump, who would not allow Cornyn to go along to get along.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s attempt at loyalty was not cultish enough.
label: SUBJ

text: Trump could still see McConnell and the Bushes and Rove through him.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s rise and fall is the story of the rise and fall of the Republican party that, whatever else might be said about it, was a political party.
label: SUBJ

text: From the beginning of his career, Cornyn was the man behind the man, a dull, reliable and invaluable figure upon whom parties depend.
label: SUBJ

text: Cornyn’s record as a dutiful man of the party condemned him.
label: SUBJ

text: Despite his attempt to adapt himself to Trump, his doom was to remain inescapably himself.
label: SUBJ

text: Running against an opponent ceaselessly defending himself against real criminal allegations, Cornyn’s indelible offense was being a Republican of his time, which he could never scrub to make himself acceptable.
label: SUBJ

text: Eileen Wang, who stepped down as the mayor of Arcadia earlier this month, was charged in April with one count of acting in the United States as an illegal agent of a foreign government.
label: OBJ

text: She pleaded guilty to doing the bidding of Chinese officials by sharing articles favorable to Beijing, without prior notification to the U.S. government as required by law.
label: OBJ

text: The 56-year-old was elected in November 2022 to a five-person city council, from which the mayor is selected on a rotating basis.
label: OBJ

text: Federal prosecutors say Wang’s illegal conduct occurred from late 2020 to 2022.
label: OBJ

text: Arcadia city officials and Wang’s attorneys have said it ended before she took office.
label: OBJ

text: Arcadia is located about 13 miles northeast of Los Angeles.
label: OBJ

text: The city of about 53,000 is majority Asian and has a high concentration of Chinese residents.
label: OBJ

text: Wang appeared in downtown LA federal court to enter her plea.
label: OBJ

text: The hearing included procedural questions from U.S. District Judge Wesley Hsu to ensure Wang understood her rights and the consequences of her guilty plea.
label: OBJ

text: A Mandarin interpreter was present but Wang said she did not need their assistance.
label: OBJ

text: Wang was allowed to remain out on a $25,000 bond until her sentencing on Oct. 6.
label: OBJ

text: She faces up to 10 years in prison and three years of supervised release.
label: OBJ

text: According to her plea agreement, Wang and her fiancé at the time, Yaoning “Mike” Sun, worked on behalf of government officials for the People’s Republic of China by promoting their propaganda on a website called U.S. News Center.
label: OBJ

text: Sun is serving a four-year sentence after he pleaded guilty to the same charge last October.
label: OBJ

text: He was also listed in campaign filings as the treasurer for Wang’s 2022 election campaign.
label: OBJ

text: In one instance in June 2021, a government official sent Wang a link to a letter to the editor published in the Los Angeles Times written by the consul general of the People’s Republic of China in Los Angeles.
label: OBJ

text: The piece refuted reports of the persecution, forced labor, and abuse of Uyghurs, the Turkic ethnic minority, in China’s Xinjiang province, stating, “There has never been genocide in Xinjiang or forced labor in the region’s cotton fields or any other sector.”
label: OBJ

text: Within minutes, Wang shared the link on her website.
label: OBJ

text: The U.S. and several other countries have declared that Beijing’s policies against the Uyghurs amounted to genocide and crimes against humanity.
label: OBJ

text: At the time, Wang was engaged to Sun, her attorneys said.
label: OBJ

text: She has said that relationship ended in spring 2024.
label: OBJ

text: A statement they released after her resignation references “her trust and love for apparently the wrong person who ultimately led her astray.”
label: OBJ

text: Residents and former Arcadia elected officials have said Wang should have been asked to resign after she came under FBI investigation for Sun’s case.
label: OBJ

text: Acting mayor Paul Cheng said he and other councilmembers’ hands were tied at the time.
label: OBJ

text: The city charter only empowers them to remove a fellow councilmember if they’ve been convicted of a crime, he said, which Wang had not at the time.
label: OBJ

text: The shooting at a mosque and school in San Diego has forced Muslim Americans to ask themselves painful questions.
label: OBJ

text: After the killing of three people in an armed attack last week, they now wonder if other places of worship will be targeted next, whether they can still send children to school and trust that they will return home unharmed, and whether they can still safely walk the streets as people identifiable by their faith.
label: OBJ

text: These are also questions that Jewish communities are reckoning with, most recently after the stabbings in London’s Golders Green neighborhood.
label: OBJ

text: Over the past three years, against the backdrop of wars in the Middle East, antisemitism and anti-Muslim hate have flared across the west, with each rising to record levels.
label: OBJ

text: But these two hatreds have rarely been seen as related dangers, let alone confronted as a common threat to societies.
label: SUBJ

text: On the weekend before the San Diego attack, tens of thousands rallied in London behind the anti-Muslim agitator Tommy Robinson, who declared a “battle of Britain” and called for “remigration”.
label: OBJ

text: “It’s time for many Muslims to leave this country,” he said.
label: OBJ

text: Across the west, as support for the far right surges, hostility towards Islam and Muslims has become central to its political platforms, and has spread beyond it.
label: OBJ

text: When Muslims prayed publicly in London’s Trafalgar Square in March to mark Ramadan – just as other religions have done on their own holy days – leading Conservative politicians denounced it as an act of “intimidation” and “domination”.
label: OBJ

text: The violence in San Diego came out of the demonization of Islam and the dehumanization of Muslims that has been around for decades – by politicians, in the media, in popular culture and across social media.
label: SUBJ

text: Islam is now widely, and even casually, described as a backward or inherently violent religion that represents a civilizational threat.
label: OBJ

text: Meanwhile, Muslims are portrayed as people whose customs and values are irreconcilable with western ones.
label: OBJ

text: They are cast as a threat to the majority’s identity, culture, security and demography.
label: OBJ

text: Antisemitism has its deep roots in vile conspiracy theories about hidden power, claiming that Jews form a shadowy elite that manipulates events through the secret control of governments, banks, the media and courts.
label: SUBJ

text: These libels are centuries-old, and they persist today.
label: OBJ

text: George Soros – a Holocaust survivor and the founder of the philanthropic organization I lead, the Open Society Foundations – is a frequent target of antisemitic attacks that deploy ugly tropes to allege his human rights philanthropy is a plot to subvert societies.
label: SUBJ

text: In 2018, these conspiracy theories led to a pipe bomb being sent to his home and were used by an attacker to justify the shooting at the Tree of Life synagogue in Pittsburgh.
label: OBJ

text: Sometimes, anti-Muslim conspiracy theories fuse with antisemitic ones.
label: OBJ

text: The clearest case is with the white nationalist “great replacement theory”, conjured up by the French polemicist Renaud Camus, who falsely claims that a conspiratorial elite is replacing white majority populations with non-whites, mostly from Muslim backgrounds.
label: SUBJ

text: The term “replacist elites” is used as a code for Jews.
label: OBJ

text: In 2017, white nationalists marched through Charlottesville, Virginia, chanting “Jews will not replace us.”
label: OBJ

text: Nigel Farage accused Soros of encouraging people to “flood Europe” and claimed Soros didn’t want the continent “to be based on Christianity”.
label: OBJ

text: It’s a single conspiracy theory that requires two elements at once: a Muslim population to fear, and a Jewish elite to blame.
label: SUBJ

text: There are also echoes across time.
label: OBJ

text: The anti-immigration campaigns of today carry reminders of the antisemitic laws that were imposed in the UK and the US in the early 20th century to prevent Jews fleeing persecution in eastern Europe from finding refuge, including after the Holocaust.
label: SUBJ

text: The “Aliens Act” of 1905 was the first British law to restrict immigration, with champions of the legislation describing Jewish people as “a race apart” and warning of the need to “push back this intolerable invasion”.
label: OBJ

text: Far-right groups marched into neighborhoods, claiming jobs were being taken from them.
label: OBJ

text: The press attacked Jewish communities for the “foreign” languages they spoke and the customs they practiced.
label: OBJ

text: Today, the two communities are frequently pitted against each other.
label: OBJ

text: When Zohran Mamdani was campaigning to become New York City’s first Muslim mayor, there was a torrent of hate directed at his identity, sometimes framed as concern for Jewish safety.
label: OBJ

text: In Germany, the chancellor has claimed that antisemitism has been “imported” by migrants, ignoring his own country’s history.
label: SUBJ

text: And in France, Marine Le Pen – whose party has antisemitic roots – says her National Rally is a shield to protect Jewish people from “Islamist ideology”.
label: OBJ

text: In each case, the message was the same: for one community to be safe, the other must be feared.
label: SUBJ

text: These divisions have been deepening since the 7 October 2023 massacre of Israelis and through the wars on Gaza, Lebanon and Iran.
label: SUBJ

text: There has been a dangerous blaming of Jewish people for the crimes of the Israeli government and of Muslims for the crimes of Hamas and other armed groups.
label: SUBJ

text: There must be space for the legitimate criticism of any state, government or ideology, but collective blame – the holding of a whole people responsible for the actions of an extreme few – must be refused.
label: SUBJ

text: We see that refusal in the response of Jewish communities in San Diego, who have been among the first to condemn the attack and stand in solidarity with Muslims.
label: SUBJ

text: We’ve seen it in the wake of the massacre at the Tree of Life synagogue, when Muslim Americans raised funds for the grieving congregation.
label: OBJ

text: When gunmen attacked a Hanukkah gathering at Sydney’s Bondi Beach last year, the man who ran at one of them and wrestled away his weapon was a Syrian-born Muslim, Ahmed al-Ahmed.
label: OBJ

text: These moments show how the defense of one community is strengthened, not weakened, when extended to the other.
label: SUBJ

text: These are the stories we must tell, and the lessons we must learn from.
label: SUBJ

text: These forms of solidarity are the foundation of a different vision – not a society organized by fear, where people are targeted for who they are and old hatreds are weaponized to decide who belongs and who does not.
label: SUBJ

text: If these hatreds rise together, feeding on conspiracy theories and the politics of fear, they cannot be defeated apart.
label: SUBJ

text: The pernicious bargain that insists on trading in the safety of one community for the rejection of another is a false one.
label: SUBJ

text: The danger does not end with Muslims and Jews.
label: SUBJ

text: The threats to these communities today will follow others tomorrow.
label: SUBJ

text: To defend them, and to defend them together, is how an open society defends itself.
label: OBJ

text: Binaifer Nowrojee is president of the Open Society Foundations
label: OBJ

text: The MAGA fanbase erupted in fury after country superstar Martina McBride announced on Thursday that she is backing out of The Great American State Fair, a President Donald Trump-aligned event she claimed she was misled about.
label: OBJ

text: “I was presented with an opportunity to perform at a nonpartisan event but that turned out to be misleading,” McBride said in an announcement on Thursday.
label: OBJ

text: “I asked lots of questions and was assured this was a nonpartisan event that was meant to celebrate ALL 50 states.”
label: OBJ

text: McBride joins a growing list of performers who are dropping out at the event scheduled to be held at the National Mall and organized by the self-described “non-partisan” Freedom 250 organization created via a presidential order.
label: SUBJ

text: The singer wrote that she initially believed “this was a great way to celebrate the states and also bring people together in the way that only music can,” and “saw it as just a bigger version of so many state fairs.” “Sounds fun, right? Wholesome even.
label: OBJ

text: “Sounds fun, right? Wholesome even. Yesterday things started changing and what we were told is, in fact, not what is happening,” McBride wrote.
label: OBJ

text: “I’ve spent my entire career singing songs about real people with real issues.
label: OBJ

text: I’ve been blessed with the opportunity to be a voice for those who have felt like they didn’t have one,” she continued.
label: OBJ

text: “It greatly upsets me that any fan who has been moved by my music may now feel like I’m abandoning the meaning behind those songs.
label: OBJ

text: I assure you, that is not the case.”
label: OBJ

text: McBride’s statement and withdrawal from the event sparked anger amongst those loyal to Trump, including Richard Grenell, the president’s former acting director of national intelligence, who took to X and slammed the country singer.
label: OBJ

text: “You’ve always been a woke Lefty,” Grenell wrote.
label: OBJ

text: Other right-wing accounts came in droves to pile on the artist.
label: SUBJ

text: “What a coward,” conservative influencer Tomi Lahren replied.
label: OBJ

text: “Imagine being this weak,” right-wing page Catturd said on X.
label: OBJ

text: Notorious MAGA-friendly page Libs of TikTok also called McBride “pathetic.”
label: SUBJ

text: However, despite the backlash from conservatives, McBride also received a round of support from others online.
label: OBJ

text: “Martina, you are a beautiful artist, mother, citizen, American.
label: SUBJ

text: You have always been exactly as you are in this moment… authentic and real.
label: SUBJ

text: So moved by you and proud to know you.”
label: OBJ

text: Singer-songwriter Sheryl Crow replied to McBride’s Instagram post.
label: OBJ

text: Others chimed in as well:
label: OBJ

text: I vote by mail in every election these days, as is my right as a mostly lazy natural-born American citizen.
label: SUBJ

text: Fill in a few bubbles with black ink, chuck the thing into the nearest dropbox, and consider myself a functioning member of society for a brief moment.
label: SUBJ

text: Now that my son is old enough to ask me coherent questions about my daily life, he was highly interested in what the hell I was doing as I marked the form.
label: SUBJ

text: “I’m voting,” I said tersely, lest I divert my attention fully from the bubble-filling.
label: OBJ

text: “Don’t vote for Spencer Pratt, Daddy,” he responded.
label: OBJ

text: “I hear he’s a jerk.”
label: OBJ

text: The word seems to be spreading.
label: SUBJ

text: Every local TV station and streaming app is turgid and bloated with political ads these days.
label: SUBJ

text: My son might be old enough to ask me who I’m voting for, but he’s not old enough to understand why.
label: SUBJ

text: That doesn’t stop campaigns from serving him countless commercials pleading with him to consider (or reconsider) a certain candidate.
label: OBJ

text: He’s now nominally aware of allegations of sexual misconduct against LA city controller Kenneth Mejia (which Mejia has denied) and the Orange County congressman Ken Calvert’s run-in with a sex worker.
label: SUBJ

text: What a joy it is to be a parent in 2026.
label: OBJ

text: But what really stuck with him is Spencer Pratt, the former star of The Hills, and candidate for mayor of Los Angeles.
label: OBJ

text: Something about Pratt sparks a bogeyman-like response in my son.
label: SUBJ

text: Perhaps it’s Pratt’s scrunched-up face, ever in a vapid grin, looking like he’s trying to sell you a car with a missing engine block.
label: SUBJ

text: It could be the increasingly scary rhetoric in the commercials that link Pratt to Donald Trump’s disastrous policies.
label: SUBJ

text: Maybe it’s his horrendous fashion sense.
label: SUBJ

text: No one should be wearing a Hugo Boss suit with Vans.
label: SUBJ

text: Looking like Burt Ward from the original Batman TV show is not a reason to shun a political candidate, I tell my son.
label: SUBJ

text: Looks are not all that matters, even if I literally just made fun of how Spencer Pratt dresses.
label: SUBJ

text: Do as I say, not as I say in my column.
label: SUBJ

text: I say listen, instead, to the family of Spencer Pratt.
label: SUBJ

text: “A vote for him is a vote for stupidity,” his sister Stephanie Pratt said back in February of this year.
label: OBJ

text: Of course, she retracted all of that in a statement to Vanity Fair this week.
label: OBJ

text: I suppose Spencer Pratt is kind of like the movie The Big Lebowski, misunderstood in its time.
label: SUBJ

text: It took decades for people to appreciate that movie.
label: OBJ

text: It only took Stephanie Pratt a couple of months and a few strong polls to come around on her brother.
label: SUBJ

text: Numerous big-money donors are rolling in to support Pratt’s campaign, which generally revolves around his personal anger at his house burning down in the 2025 Palisades fire.
label: SUBJ

text: Also, the hot-button issue of stepping on poop on the street.
label: OBJ

text: “Vote pratt if you’re tired of human poop on the streets,” as the candidate put it, is a campaign rallying cry both hyper-specific to one person’s experience (Pratt shared a post on X about witnessing an unhoused person defecating near a BMW) and intensely relatable.
label: SUBJ

text: I, too, have seen a person poop in public.
label: OBJ

text: I did not care for it.
label: OBJ

text: That, unfortunately, is the core of Pratt’s appeal here in Los Angeles.
label: SUBJ

text: People, especially rich ones, are sick of the city not working the way they want it to.
label: SUBJ

text: The wildfires that devastated neighborhoods on both sides of town galvanized frustrated citizens in both ideological camps, creating an intellectual dissonance that could cause our local government to become even more paralyzed.
label: SUBJ

text: People want unhoused Angelenos off the street, but they don’t want them housed in their neighborhoods.
label: SUBJ

text: Pratt has blamed homelessness on drug use, not the high cost of living in a place like LA.
label: OBJ

text: His brilliant idea is to bus people without homes to Seattle.
label: SUBJ

text: Sounds like a small-scale version of a familiar policy.
label: SUBJ

text: So much of this is wrapped up in Pratt’s hilariously pandering slogan of making LA “camera-ready” again.
label: SUBJ

text: It plays perfectly into the inherent vanity of many people who live here, who are more concerned with the amount of graffiti on the 6th Street Bridge than whether a middle-class family can afford to live in that neighborhood.
label: SUBJ

text: Pratt’s taken a page directly out of the Trump handbook: emphasizing grievances, vague promises and a sense of unearned exceptionalism that flatters the egos of voters.
label: SUBJ

text: As long as you remain mad, you can ignore the candidate’s lack of experience or inability to articulate actual policy outside of “I will fix it”.
label: SUBJ

text: AI slop videos cast this unremarkable man as a superhero that looks nothing like the actual Spencer Pratt.
label: SUBJ

text: Unfortunately, Batman is not real, but if he was, he wouldn’t wear sneakers with a Hugo Boss suit.
label: SUBJ

text: Everyone knows Batman wears Armani.
label: SUBJ

text: But vapid, cheaply made fantasy propaganda is the grist of modern politics.
label: SUBJ

text: Specifics just confuse people.
label: SUBJ

text: Whatever Pratt plans to do, you have to hunt to suss it out.
label: SUBJ

text: Nowhere on Pratt’s website is there even a nod to income inequality or the cost-of-living crisis.
label: SUBJ

text: You have to go to Pratt’s Substack to learn about his policy proposals for bringing motion picture production back to LA, which requires you to wade through a screed about the upcoming Baywatch reboot.
label: SUBJ

text: The unspoken piece of this is the 2028 Summer Olympics, a moment where literal TV cameras will be filming the city night and day.
label: SUBJ

text: Like an actor going on a harsh diet before shooting a Marvel movie, LA is in the throes of a crisis of self-esteem that all of us here can relate to.
label: SUBJ

text: Our problems – inefficient transportation, high rents, a massive bureaucracy that doesn’t respond to the needs of the people – have been festering for decades now.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s just that the rest of the world is about to find out about all those unsightly blemishes.
label: SUBJ

text: As if he’s some dark huckster self-help guru, Spencer Pratt has descended on LA to gas us up and tell us we can be beautiful again if we just quit carbs and inject this needle in our metaphorical faces.
label: SUBJ

text: The reality that the mayor’s power in Los Angeles is limited is immaterial to the big-money donors and angry citizenry.
label: SUBJ

text: They just want the facelift, even if they can’t afford it.
label: SUBJ

text: If Spencer Pratt, who makes 2016 Donald Trump seem a highly experienced politician, is elected mayor this year, he will have the same tools Karen Bass, Eric Garcetti Antonio Villaraigosa, and everyone before him had to try to change things.
label: SUBJ

text: He’ll have to coerce and cajole city councilpeople, bureaucrats he can’t fire and special interests who don’t want to give up their piece of the pie.
label: SUBJ

text: If he can’t even convince my eight-year-old, I’m not sure what hope he has.
label: SUBJ

text: On top of all that, he never even apologized to LC. Unforgivable.
label: SUBJ

text: The setting sun was framed by a canyon of skyscrapers Thursday as it sunk below the horizon, perfectly aligned with the Manhattan street grid.
label: OBJ

text: The dramatic spectacle was just the first of the year.
label: SUBJ

text: Another version of the setting sun was possible between New York’s famed skyscrapers on Friday.
label: OBJ

text: The phenomenon then repeats on July 11 and 12.
label: OBJ

text: Manhattanhenge happens about three weeks before and after the summer solstice.
label: OBJ

text: Over the years, it has become a must-see event, bringing photographers and others out onto the city sidewalks on spring and summer evenings.
label: SUBJ

text: Some background on the uniquely New York experience:
label: SUBJ

text: Where does the name Manhattanhenge come from?
label: OBJ

text: Astrophysicist Neil deGrasse Tyson coined the term in a 1997 article in the magazine “Natural History.”
label: OBJ

text: Tyson, the director of the Hayden Planetarium at New York’s American Museum of Natural History, said he was inspired by a visit to Stonehenge as a teenager.
label: OBJ

text: The future host of TV shows such as PBS’ “Nova ScienceNow” was part of an expedition led by Gerald Hawkins, the scientist who first theorized that Stonehenge’s mysterious megaliths were an ancient astronomical observatory.
label: OBJ

text: It struck Tyson, a native New Yorker, that the setting sun framed by Manhattan’s high-rises could be compared to the sun’s rays striking the center of the Stonehenge circle on the solstice.
label: OBJ

text: Unlike the Neolithic Stonehenge builders, the planners who laid out Manhattan did not mean to channel the sun.
label: OBJ

text: It just worked out that way. When is Manhattanhenge?
label: OBJ

text: When is Manhattanhenge? Manhattanhenge does not take place on the summer solstice itself, which is June 21 this year.
label: OBJ

text: Instead, it happens about three weeks before and after the solstice.
label: OBJ

text: That’s when the sun aligns itself perfectly with the Manhattan grid’s east-west streets.
label: OBJ

text: Viewers get to choose between two different versions of the phenomenon.
label: OBJ

text: On Thursday, and again on July 12, half the sun is above the horizon and half below it at the moment of alignment with Manhattan’s streets, according to the Hayden Planetarium.
label: OBJ

text: On Friday and July 11, the whole sun can appear to hover between buildings just before sinking into the New Jersey horizon across the Hudson River.
label: OBJ

text: Where can you see Manhattanhenge?
label: SUBJ

text: The traditional viewing spots are along the city’s broad east-west thoroughfares: 14th Street, 23rd Street, 34th Street, 42nd Street and 57th Street.
label: OBJ

text: The farther east you go, the more dramatic the vista as the sun hits building facades on either side of the street.
label: SUBJ

text: It is also possible to see Manhattanhenge across the East River in the Long Island City section of Queens.
label: OBJ

text: Is Manhattanhenge an organized event? No, not really.
label: SUBJ

text: No, not really. Seeing Manhattanhenge is mostly a DIY affair.
label: OBJ

text: People gather on east-west streets a half-hour or so before sunset and snap photos as dusk approaches.
label: OBJ

text: That’s if the weather is fine.
label: OBJ

text: There’s no visible Manhattanhenge on rainy or cloudy days.
label: OBJ

text: Do other cities have similar sunset events?
label: SUBJ

text: Similar effects occur in other cities with uniform street grids.
label: OBJ

text: Chicagohenge and Baltimorehenge happen when the setting sun lines up with the gridded streets in those cities in March and September, around the spring and fall equinoxes.
label: OBJ

text: Torontohenge occurs in February and October.
label: OBJ

text: But Manhattanhenge is particularly striking because of the height of the buildings and the unobstructed path to the Hudson River.
label: SUBJ

text: Donald Trump has been facing a lot of allegations that he’s snoozing on the job.
label: OBJ

text: But we should give the poor man a break: he must be exhausted by his unceasing efforts to make life better for us all.
label: SUBJ

text: At this very moment, for example, the Trump administration is spending $5m to cover four bronze horses near the Lincoln Memorial in thick gold leaf.
label: OBJ

text: No longer will passersby be subjected to subpar equine aesthetics.
label: SUBJ

text: Finally, the American people will have the glimmering horse statues they deserve.
label: SUBJ

text: Meanwhile, the US has been fighting a war with Iran that, by one expert’s estimate, is costing $2bn dollars a day and will probably end up with a price tag of at least a trillion dollars.
label: OBJ

text: This may seem like a colossal waste of money to some, but real patriots understand that this is simply the cost of making America great again.
label: SUBJ

text: Alas, President Trump isn’t getting the appreciation he deserves.
label: SUBJ

text: Instead of looking at the bigger picture (eg the strategic importance of enriching billionaires), people keep asking stupid questions like: “Why can’t I afford my groceries?”
label: SUBJ

text: and “Why did my electric bill just double?”
label: SUBJ

text: Trump has been sinking in the polls for a while now, recently hitting record new lows.
label: SUBJ

text: Affordability is a key driver of negative sentiment, with a majority of Americans saying that the Iran war has made life a lot more expensive for them.
label: OBJ

text: Image-obsessed Trump can’t be thrilled by his plummeting poll numbers.
label: SUBJ

text: But what should really frighten him is this: he’s hemorrhaging support from young men, a demographic that helped him win his second term.
label: SUBJ

text: Back in February, the centrist thinktank Third Way released a poll that found 66% of young men disapproved of Trump’s performance in office.
label: OBJ

text: Meanwhile, influential podcast bros like Joe Rogan and Theo Von, who played a key part in getting Trump elected, have publicly soured on the president.
label: SUBJ

text: In April, Von called Trump’s attacks on Iran “diabolical”, asking: “What regular person is this helping? ... I don’t understand.”
label: OBJ

text: ... I don’t understand.” Rogan has also criticized Trump’s handling of the Iran war, noting that one reason so many people voted for Trump was because he promised “no more wars”.
label: OBJ

text: Trump’s decreasing support from young men, and increased criticism from manosphere-adjacent podcasters, isn’t new.
label: OBJ

text: However, it’s getting more scrutiny as preparations ramp up for the 14 June Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC) match on the White House lawn.
label: OBJ

text: The UFC’s core demographic is young men and UFC events played an important role in Trump’s campaign strategy.
label: SUBJ

text: During a CNN discussion about the event on Thursday, the host, Kate Bolduan, was so shocked by Trump’s approval rating drop among men under 30 that she initially thought a graph shown by data expert Harry Enten must be incorrect.
label: OBJ

text: “No, that is not a typo,” Enten said.
label: OBJ

text: “That is a 56-point move against the president of the United States among young men who helped put him over the top in the election.
label: OBJ

text: And now he is very much struggling with them.
label: OBJ

text: Maybe that’s part of the reason why he wants to put on an event like this, to try and connect with some of them.”
label: SUBJ

text: I mean, yes, that’s obviously part of the reason why this ridiculous event is going ahead.
label: SUBJ

text: But Trump is going to have to do a lot better than a cage fight to win back disillusioned young men.
label: SUBJ

text: Even Rogan, who has been a UFC commentator for decades and who is expected to be at the event, has his reservations about the fight.
label: OBJ

text: During a March podcast, he said he was excited but it was “weird to have a fight at the White House in the middle of a fucking war”.
label: OBJ

text: He added: “I would hope the war will be sorted out by June, but quite honestly, I’m not confident that’s going to be the case.”
label: OBJ

text: Even if the war is miraculously sorted out by June, it’s already done a hell of a lot of damage to the global economy and US consumers.
label: SUBJ

text: And we are likely to see even higher food prices because of a completely unnecessary war.
label: SUBJ

text: You can’t just appease the people with circuses; you’ve also got to give them affordable bread.
label: SUBJ

text: All this said, I wouldn’t take much satisfaction from the dissatisfaction in Trump among young men.
label: SUBJ

text: I have a horrible feeling that the Democrats, who put together a $20m plan last year to build a “liberal Joe Rogan”, have learned all the wrong lessons from young men’s lurch to the right.
label: SUBJ

text: Gavin Newsom, for example, widely considered a top contender for the 2028 Democratic nomination, has been aggressively leaning into bro culture.
label: SUBJ

text: He has even adopted manosphere language; in July, he called Stephen Miller a “fascist cuck”.
label: SUBJ

text: Should Democrats empathize with economically anxious young men? Of course.
label: SUBJ

text: Of course. But they should not coddle angry men who have decided that women are to blame for all their problems.
label: SUBJ

text: An extraordinary number of people appear to think otherwise.
label: SUBJ

text: Richard Reeves, the president of the American Institute for Boys and Men, a left-leaning research institute, recently told the New York Times that he was exasperated by the seemingly ubiquitous opinion that young men are now all rightwing “Andrew Tate-style misogynists”.
label: OBJ

text: “Maybe,” Reeves said, “they’re actually really swingy and up for grabs.”
label: OBJ

text: Please, can we shout that from the rooftops?
label: SUBJ

text: Winning young men does not have to mean shifting to the right and calling people cucks on the internet.
label: SUBJ

text: It doesn’t have to mean indulging misogynistic ideas.
label: SUBJ

text: Zohran Mamdani, and his very healthy masculinity, managed to attract men and women alike: Mamdani won 65% of young men compared with the 27% who voted for Andrew Cuomo.
label: SUBJ

text: If only the Democrats could learn from Mamdani, instead of trying to manufacture a manosphere of their own.
label: SUBJ

text: It’s difficult to describe the level of optimism I had for the future of our country in the days, weeks, and months after Donald Trump’s improbable victory over Kamala Harris in 2024.
label: OBJ

text: After the Covid-fueled mess that ended Trump’s first term, the political fallout surrounding the J-6 riots, and four disastrous years under a dementia-riddled Joe Biden during which the border remained wide open for all comers, another four years under Harris would have likely sealed America’s fate.

text: It was the coalition to end all coalitions, as least insofar as our fractured politics has become.
label: SUBJ

text: In addition to a solid MAGA base that would have followed Trump into the ocean if he chose to walk there, it was an election won by disaffected Democrats, by MAHA moms, by non-interventionist libertarians, by minorities fed up with empty Democrat promises, by podcast bros like Joe Rogan and formerly center-left cultural figures like Elon Musk who seemingly overnight found themselves considered by the left to be far right for supporting positions liberals believed a decade ago, and by countless ordinary Americans just tired of the craziness.

text: The Navy on Monday confirmed that the wreckage of a World War II-era submarine discovered off the coast of an uninhabited volcanic island in the Russian-controlled Kuril archipelago near Japan is the USS Herring, which was lost in June 1944.
label: OBJ

text: The submarine rests at a depth of more than 300 feet and sits upright on its keel.
label: OBJ

text: Navy officials said the Herring maintains “a high degree of integrity.”
label: OBJ

text: “The submarine displays battle damage around the conning tower and evidence of grounding at her bow, correlating with the historical record,” the Navy said in a statement Monday.
label: OBJ

text: The Herring was launched on Jan. 15, 1942, and commissioned into service four months later.
label: OBJ

text: Before her loss, she completed eight war patrols in the Atlantic and Pacific theaters and is credited with sinking seven enemy ships, including four Japanese cargo ships during her final patrol.
label: OBJ

text: The Herring was last seen on May 31, 1944, by the crew of another submarine, the USS Barb, when they met to coordinate patrol areas off the Kuril Islands.
label: OBJ

text: On June 1, the Barb recorded the sound of distant depth charges exploding and took it as evidence of an attack against the USS Herring, Navy officials said.
label: OBJ

text: Shore batteries also scored two direct hits on the sub’s conning tower after it had sunk two Japanese ships, the Ishigaki Maru and the Hokuyo Maru.
label: OBJ

text: The USS Herring was presumed lost when she failed to report to Navy officials on Midway in July 1944, officials said.
label: OBJ

text: The Kuril Islands have been under Russian administration since their 1945 invasion by the Soviet Union near the end of World War II.
label: OBJ

text: The wreckage was discovered in 2017 during a joint expedition by the Russian Geographic Society and the Russian Navy.
label: OBJ

text: A commemorative plaque was placed at the site during a later exploration in 2022.
label: OBJ

text: The Naval History and Research Command confirmed the wreck site as the final resting place of the USS Herring using data collected by the Russians and analyzed by U.S. and Japanese researchers.
label: OBJ

text: The U.S. considers it a protected area and said any research activity that might disturb the wreckage should be coordinated by U.S. Navy officials.
label: OBJ

text: “More importantly, the wreck represents the final resting place of sailors who gave their lives in defense of the nation and should be respected by all parties as a war grave,” Navy officials said.

text: Congressional incumbents this primary cycle are getting pounded by a perfect storm of redistricting-based competition, Democrats pushing for generational change and President Trump wading into GOP contests.
label: SUBJ

text: The latter has proved the most powerful at this point in the 2026 cycle, as Trump-backed challengers have ousted three of the six members of Congress who have lost their primaries.
label: OBJ

text: “The president’s intervention in primaries is very unusual, and frankly, very effective for a sitting president,” election analyst Kyle Kondik told [OUTLET].
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Kondik, managing editor of Sabato’s Crystal Ball at the University of Virginia Center for Politics, said Republican Sens. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana and John Cornyn of Texas “were in trouble anyway, but certainly Trump weighing in against them didn’t really help.”
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Cassidy and Mr. Cornyn, who have respectively served two and four terms, are the first elected incumbents to lose a Senate primary in 14 years.
label: OBJ

text: Since 2012, Senate Republicans have lost only eight incumbents total to electoral defeats.
label: OBJ

text: Half of those came in 2020 when Democrats regained control of the chamber and Joseph R. Biden beat Mr. Trump in the presidential election.
label: OBJ

text: Democrats are looking to oust three GOP incumbents this cycle: Sens. Susan Collins of Maine, John Husted of Ohio and Dan Sullivan of Alaska.
label: OBJ

text: It would take a blue wave to knock out all three, but if that happened, the combined five GOP general and primary losses would be the most Senate Republicans ousted since 2008.
label: SUBJ

text: Five GOP senators also lost their seats that year.
label: OBJ

text: Senate Democrats are only in danger of losing two incumbents, one in a primary and one in the general.
label: OBJ

text: Rep. Seth Moulton, 47, is challenging Sen. Edward J. Markey, 79, in the Massachusetts Democratic primary on a platform of generational change.
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Markey is leading by 5 points in the most recent poll.
label: OBJ

text: The primary is Sept. 1.
label: OBJ

text: Georgia Sen. Jon Ossoff, who has raised the most money of any Senate candidate this cycle, is the only Democratic incumbent in danger of losing in the general as others from competitive states opted to retire instead of run for reelection.
label: OBJ

text: In the House, the average number of incumbents defeated in primaries over the past 40 election cycles is 6.5, and the median is 5, according to Crystal Ball’s data analysis.
label: OBJ

text: This year will hit the median, if not the average.
label: SUBJ

text: So far, four incumbents – two Republicans and two Democrats – have lost, but that number could double as the rest of the primary season unfolds.
label: OBJ

text: Seven-term Kentucky GOP Rep. Thomas Massie was ousted by a Trump-backed challenger.
label: OBJ

text: Rep. Dan Crenshaw, a four-term Texas Republican, lost a right-wing challenge from state Rep. Steve Toth in the 2nd District.
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Trump opted against endorsing in that race, despite backing all other House GOP incumbents in Texas.
label: OBJ

text: Two Texas Democrats, Reps. Al Green and Julie Johnson, suffered defeats a few months later in the state’s primary runoff election.
label: OBJ

text: Redistricting was the culprit in Mr. Green’s case, as the Republican-redrawn map moved Mr. Green’s 9th District eastward into Republican territory.
label: SUBJ

text: Most of Mr. Green’s south Houston constituency was moved into the 18th District, represented by Rep. Christian Menefee, creating an incumbent matchup that showed Democrats’ desire for generational change.
label: SUBJ

text: Mr. Green, a 78-year-old in his 11th term, lost overwhelmingly to the newcomer 40 years his junior.
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Menefee, 38, was elected in a February special election to replace Democratic Rep. Sylvester Turner, who died last year.
label: OBJ

text: Ms. Johnson, a first-term House member, avoided a redistricting-forced matchup against fellow Democratic Rep. Marc Veasey after the new map effectively merged their Dallas-Fort Worth area districts.
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Veasey decided not to run, but Ms. Johnson’s predecessor, Colin Allred, entered the race after dropping his Senate bid, the reason he did not run for reelection to the House in 2024.
label: OBJ

text: Mr. Allred prevailed over Ms. Johnson, who became the fourth House incumbent to lose this year.
label: OBJ

text: A fifth incumbent is guaranteed to go in California as two Republicans, Reps. Young Kim and Ken Calvert, face off in the 40th District.
label: SUBJ

text: The Democrat-led redistricting in the state made Mr. Calvert’s 41st District more blue and shifted much of his GOP base into Ms. Kim’s 40th District.
label: OBJ

text: Californians head to the polls on Tuesday, but the state has an open primary system where candidates all run on the same ballot and the top two vote-getters, regardless of party, advance to the general election.
label: OBJ

text: That means Ms. Kim and Mr. Calvert could both survive until November, but one of them will not be returning to Congress next year.
label: OBJ

text: A handful of Democratic incumbents are also facing tough primaries this summer.

text: Two are coming up on June 23 when New Yorkers and Marylanders head to the polls.
label: OBJ

text: First-term Maryland Democratic Rep. April McClain Delaney is facing a primary challenge from her Sixth District predecessor, David Trone, who ran for Senate in 2024 but lost the primary to Angela Alsobrooks.

text: August primaries could also see some more incumbent upsets.

text: First-term Rep. Wesley Bell, Missouri Democrat, is trying to prevent his progressive predecessor Cori Bush from making a comeback in the 1st District.

text: The Missouri primary is Aug. 4.

text: A week later, on Aug. 11, Connecticut Rep. John B. Larson will face off against Hartford Mayor Luke Bronin, who has pulled ahead of him in the most recent poll.

text: Three Latvian climbers died and a fourth survived after a fall on Wednesday near Denali Pass on Alaska's Mount McKinley.
label: OBJ

text: Four members of a seven-person climbing expedition fell near Denali Pass, about 2,100 feet below the mountain's 20,310-foot summit, according to Reuters.
label: OBJ

text: The Latvian Mountaineering Association identified the deceased climbers as Inese Puceka, Vija Olte and Renars Kunigs-Salaks, the outlet reported.
label: OBJ

text: The association said a fourth climber, Mārtiņš Bilzēns, survived the fall and was in critical condition.
label: OBJ

text: Rescue crews from the National Park Service evacuated the surviving climber on Thursday from a basin at approximately 17,200 feet.
label: OBJ

text: Challenging terrain and weather conditions prevented a helicopter from landing.
label: OBJ

text: That required rescuers to use a long-line extraction system before transporting the climber to Kahiltna Base Camp and later to a hospital, according to the National Park Service.
label: OBJ

text: The three remaining members of the expedition who were not involved in the fall returned to High Camp after assisting their climbing partners, park officials said.
label: OBJ

text: Following the rescue efforts at high altitude, all three experienced declining physical conditions and were evacuated from the mountain Friday.
label: OBJ

text: Recovery efforts for the three climbers who died remain ongoing as weather, terrain and mountain conditions continue to affect access to the site, park officials said.
label: OBJ

text: The accident occurred near Denali Pass, one of the most hazardous sections of the mountain.
label: OBJ

text: The stretch between High Camp and Denali Pass has been the site of numerous injuries and fatalities over the years, with many incidents involving unprotected falls, according to reports.
label: OBJ

text: Mount McKinley, also known as Denali, rises about 20,310 feet above sea level and is considered one of the most demanding climbs in North America.
label: OBJ

text: The mountain's steep slopes, glaciers, rapidly changing weather and extreme altitude create significant challenges even for experienced climbers.
label: OBJ

text: Only about 1,000 to 1,200 climbers attempt to reach the summit each year, most during the May and June climbing season.
label: OBJ

text: Less than half of those who attempted the climb last year successfully reached the top, according to park statistics.
label: OBJ

text: More than 130 people have died on the mountain throughout the park's history.
label: OBJ

text: [OUTLET] reached out to the National Park Service for further comment. Reuters contributed reporting.
label: OBJ
